You will be successful if you are an experienced in-house recruiter who runs searches with sound judgement, understands when structured approaches are needed and when flexibility adds value, and can confidently influence senior stakeholders with clear recommendations and thoughtful challenge. This role suits someone who can balance breadth with depth and help shape the way talent supports Metrea’s mission.


### **What You'll Do**


* Lead end-to-end recruitment across a globally dispersed technology division, managing a mix of individual contributor, specialist, and leadership searches
* Partner with hiring managers and senior stakeholders to shape talent strategies that align with business priorities, while eagerly learning the business and educating on recruiting best practices
* Tenaciously source and build pipelines, while engaging and nurturing relationships with candidates and prospects using empathy and transparency, ensuring they’re aligned with Metrea’s mission and values
* Guide and consult with stakeholders using market knowledge and insights to ensure the best hiring decisions
* Identify opportunities to improve recruitment practices, pipelines, and candidate experience
* Manage multi-step hiring processes across global teams and time zones, ensuring a smooth and high-quality candidate experience
* Champion Metrea’s employer brand to attract mission-aligned talent

### **What You Bring**


* In-house recruitment experience in a scaling or fast-changing organisation, managing a broad range of technical roles
* Demonstrated experience recruiting for complex technical skillsets such as software engineering, product management, engineering management, UX/UI, data science, and computational engineering
* Proven ability to consult, partner, and influence, leveraging excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build trust and credibility with candidates and stakeholders alike
* Familiarity with a wide range of domain-specific talent markets, including key talent pools and industry trends
* Strong judgement in shaping recruitment approaches and improving processes to deliver high-quality hires
* Track record of building and maintaining diverse, high-quality candidate pipelines
* Experience managing multi-step, complex hiring processes across global teams and time zones
* Ability to balance breadth and depth, adapting to varying role types and business needs
* Experience with Greenhouse or other applicant tracking systems

### **Benefits**


* Friskvård of 5K annually
* Parental leave
* 5% occupational pension (swe. tjänstepension)
* 30 days of leave
* Bonus Scheme
* Personal development opportunities

### **Right to Work**


The employee will have the legal right to work in Sweden and will be able to produce right to work documentation.


### **Equal Opportunities Statement**


Metrea Management Limited (MAM) is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or other characteristics protected by law.


### **Position Type and Expected Hours of Work**


This is a full-time non-exempt position with typical working hours of Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Hours and work shifts may change in accordance with department and business needs.


### **Work Environment**


This job operates in an office setting. Hybrid; minimum of 3 days/week onsite.


### **Travel**


Less than 5%


### **Our Firmware**


At Metrea, our single core value is Rooted in Humility, and our four cornerstone attributes are Entrepreneurial, Systematic, Discerning, and Over-Deliver. These form what we call our Teammate Firmware. Just like technical firmware connects software and hardware, our Firmware is the constant interface between our mission and our people. It defines how we show up, how we work together, and how we solve complex problems.


Our team Firmware creates a web-like, hyper-collaborative, dynamically hierarchical way of working that helps us adapt quickly, communicate openly, and distribute decision-making to where expertise actually lives. It enables groups to self-organize around hard problems, shift fluidly as priorities evolve, and operate with the trust, curiosity, and discipline required in a complex mission space. This foundation allows us to deliver elegant, effective solutions and uphold our purpose: protecting our precious inheritance.


Back to all jobs

title: Customer Engagement Specialist, Ellos
description:
Ellos Group är en av Nordens ledande e-handelsgrupper med välkända varumärken som Ellos, Jotex och Homeroom samt betalvarumärket Elpy. Vi har alltid kunden i fokus och arbetar i nära samspel med miljontals kunder för att erbjuda attraktiva och hållbara lösningar inom mode och heminredning. Genom innovation, kreativitet och hållbarhet söker vi ständigt nya vägar framåt, både stort och smått. Vårt huvudkontor ligger i Borås och vi är verksamma i hela Norden och på utvalda europeiska marknader. Idag är vi cirka 500 engagerade medarbetare och omsätter omkring 3,5 miljarder SEK.

### **Customer Engagement Specialist, Ellos**

#### **Om rollen**


På Ellos arbetar vi just nu med att vidareutveckla vår kundkommunikation och söker dig som vill vara med och skapa engagerande och personliga kundresor. Du har ett par års erfarenhet inom CRM, ett skarpt öga för hur rätt budskap når rätt person vid rätt tillfälle, samt nyfikenhet och mod som gör att du vågar ifrågasätta invanda strukturer.


Du trivs i en roll där du får driva framåt, utforska nya möjligheter och ständigt optimera kommunikationen. Hos oss arbetar du i Bloomreach, en av marknadens mest kraftfulla MarTech-plattformar


Som Customer Engagement Specialist kommer du att arbeta med att skapa engagerande e-post- och sms-kampanjer med fokus på personalisering. Du arbetar i tätt samarbete med CRM Manager och agerar brygga mellan strategi och operativt genomförande, samt bidrar till att utveckla Ellos kundkommunikation över tid.

#### **Ansvarsområden**


Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter inkluderar:

* Planera och producera relevanta e-post- och sms-kampanjer med fokus på personalisering och ett skarpt öga för hur rätt budskap ska nå rätt person vid rätt tillfälle
* Koordinera aktiviteter i tätt samarbete med kollegor inom marknadsavdelningen
* Arbeta datadrivet med analys och optimering av kundresor
* Bidra till utvecklingen av Ellos CRM-arbete med utgångspunkt i CRM-strategin

#### **Som person tror vi att du är**

* Högskoleutbildning inom ekonomi/marknadsföring/e-commerce samt erfarenhet från liknande roll inom CRM, kundkommunikation, dataanalys, UX/CRO.
* God kompetens och intresse av e-handel och kundbeteenden
* Vana att arbeta med marknadsföringverktyg och ad-copybeskrivning
* Erfarenhet inom planering och koordinering med fokus på personalisering
* Stark analytisk förmåga – du trivs med att arbeta med kunddata och dra affärsinsikter
* God kommunikativ förmåga på både svenska och engelska


Du är nyfiken, engagerad och gillar att ta ansvar för både detaljer och helhet. Du är intresserad av hur personalisering, data och AI kan användas för att skapa bättre kundupplevelser, och är inte rädd för att testa nytt. Du samarbetar enkelt över funktioner och motiveras av att ständigt förbättra kundupplevelsen.


Noggrannhet och kvalitet genomsyrar ditt arbetssätt, samtidigt som du är flexibel och anpassningsbar i en föränderlig miljö.

**Vad kan Ellos erbjuda dig?**  

Hos Ellos blir du en del av ett engagerat och datadrivet team med fokus på att leverera den bästa möjliga kundupplevelsen till kvinnan mitt i livet. Du får:

* En unik möjlighet att bli en del av Ellos Group – en av de tre största e-handelsaktörerna i Sverige
* På Ellos befinner du dig i framkant av e-handelsinnovation
* Möjlighet att arbeta och utvecklas tillsammans med engagerade och kunniga kollegor
* En arbetsplats med fokus på trivsel och balans mellan jobb och fritid, samt utvecklingsmöjligheter
* Personalrabatt erbjuds i alla våra e-handelsbutiker

#### **Rekryteringsprocessen**


Vill du forma framtidens digitala arbetsplats med oss?


Skicka in din ansökan snarast via länken, **dock senast den 3 juli.** Vi tar inte emot ansökningar via mejl. Urval sker löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan ansökningstiden går ut. Vår rekryteringsprocess är strikt kompetensbaserad. Därför ber vi dig att inte bifoga foton eller inkludera din ålder i din ansökan.  

  

Vi ser fram emot att lära känna dig.

#### **Ellos Group som arbetsgivare**


Hos oss är du en del av ett innovativt team som bygger framtidens e-handel och arbetsplats. Vår kultur präglas av värderingarna Ödmjuka vinnare, Entreprenörskap och Glädje – en miljö där varje medarbetare får växa, utmana sig själv och bidra till utveckling. Du ges möjlighet att utmana invanda processer och arbeta med både stabilitet och nytänkande som grund.

* Stort fokus på friskvård inom vår interna friskvårdsatsning Ellos Group Movement samt tillgång till gym och subventionerad massage på vårt huvudkontor i Borås.
* Personalrabatter och andra attraktiva förmåner.
* En kreativ arbetsmiljö där vi utmanar och inspirerar varandra.

**Lika rättigheter och möjligheter**


Inom Ellos Group eftersträvar vi att erbjuda en inkluderande arbetsplats där alla känner sig respekterade och välkomna. Alla arbetssökande och anställda behandlas lika oavsett kön, etnisk tillhörighet, religion, sexuell läggning, ålder, funktionsnedsättning eller andra faktorer.

* Sista ansökningsdag: 3 Juli
* Önskat startdatum: 28 September
* Kontrakt/Anställningsform: Tillsvidare
* **Location:** Borås (Hybrid available)

title: CRO & Analytics Specialist
description:
**Om oss**  

På TRKKN drivs vi av att lyfta våra kunders data-drivna resa till nya höjder. Som Europas ledande Google-partner och återförsäljare av Google Cloud och Google Marketing Platform fungerar vi som en katalysator för banbrytande digitala marknadsföringslösningar.


Vårt team är mångfaldigt, internationellt och inspirerande, och vi verkar i en snabbt växande digital och teknologisk miljö. Detta ger en dynamisk arbetsplats för individer som drivs av en hunger efter tillväxt, ansvar, och en önskan att forma sin egen framtid. Med en nära relation till Google är möjligheterna för lärande och utveckling på TRKKN stora.


På TRKKN Sverige är vi ett lokalt team av 4 personer och befinner oss i en expansiv tillväxtfas där du får vara med och bygga TRKKN Sverige. Vi har ett nära samarbete med våra systerbyråer OMD, PHD, Hearts & Science, UM, Initiative, TransAct, Annalect, Fuse och Drum som tillsammans består av ca 320 fantastiska medarbete på den svenska marknaden.

 **Om tjänsten**  

Som CRO och webbanalys-specialist kommer du att vara en viktig del i att driva konverteringsoptimering och webbanalys för TRKKNs kunder genom operativt arbete likaväl som införsäljning och produktutveckling. Detta inkluderar bland annat:

* Arbeta operativt och strategiskt med optimeringen av våra kunders webbplatser och plattformar
* Vara en specialist inom CRO och webbanalys och stödja kunderna i hela CRO-processen från datainsamling till uppsättning av tester och analyser med ett proaktivt förhållningssätt
* Bygga interna relationer likaväl som kundrelationer för att möjliggöra införsäljning av nya kunduppdrag
* Arbeta tillsammans med teamet för att hitta nya produkter och lösningar på våra kunders utmaningar

**Vem är du?**


Vi söker dig som är analytisk, tekniskt nyfiken och som trivs i en konsultiv roll med mycket kundkontakt. Du motiveras av att förstå olika branschers utmaningar och förvandla data till konkreta affärsvärden.

**Vi tror att du har:**

* Utbildning: Relevant eftergymnasial utbildning inom digital marknadsföring, dataanalys, UX eller motsvarande.
* Erfarenhet: Några års erfarenhet av att arbeta med webbanalys, CRO och UX.
* Verktygskoll: Mycket goda kunskaper i Google Analytics 4 (GA4) och Google Tag Manager (GTM).
* Teknisk förståelse: Goda kunskaper i frontend-kodning och att du känner dig bekväm med HTML, CSS och JavaScript för att kunna sätta upp experiment själv.
* Experimentering: Erfarenhet av experimenteringsverktyg som exempelvis *Convert*, *Optimizely* eller liknande.

**Meriterande om du har:**

* Erfarenhet av verktyg inom Google Marketing Platform (GMP).
* Erfarenhet av molnlösningar (Google Cloud Platform / BigQuery).
* Kunskaper i Instant BQML eller prediktiv analys.
* Erfarenhet av implementering av AI-lösningar.

 **Vi erbjuder dig**

* En riktigt rolig arbetsplats med möjlighet att samarbeta med några av världens mest innovativa och spännande varumärken.
* En uppsjö av afterworks, konferensresor, event och roliga aktiviteter som gör att vi lär känna varandra både som kollegor och vänner.
* Förmåner som tjänstepension och försäkringar, friskvårdsbidrag med tillgång till förmånsportalen Nordea Node.
* 37,5 timmars arbetsvecka med flexibelt kontorsarbete.
* Nyrenoverande lokaler på Södermalm med Stockholms bästa utsikt över Gamla stan och Djurgården.

**Intresserad?**  

Vi hoppas att du vill bli en del av vår fortsatta resa och ser fram emot att lära känna dig bättre. Vi intervjuar löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista svarsdatum. För frågor om tjänsten, varmt välkommen att kontakta Evelina Jörhov, Head of TRKKN Sverige, evelina.jorhov@omc.com.


Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Senior Data Engineer - Databricks Platform (Azure)
description:
***This is us***  

  

At Avenga, we believe that human creativity empowers technology that matters. Operating globally, our 6000+ specialists provide a full spectrum of services, including business and tech advisory, enterprise solutions, CX, UX and Ul design, managed services, product development, and software development.  

  



***This is the job***

  

For our client in the banking sector, we are looking for a Senior Data Engineer to help build and evolve a modern cloud-based data platform powered by Databricks and Microsoft Azure.  

  

The client is undergoing a major transformation from a traditional on-premise SQL-based data warehouse to a scalable, cloud-native data ecosystem where Databricks serves as the core platform for data processing, analytics, and future AI initiatives.


This role is ideal for engineers who have extensive hands-on experience working with Databricks in production environments and enjoy building robust, scalable data platforms from the ground up.

 ***This is you***

* 5+ years of experience in Data Engineering
* 3-5+ years of hands-on Databricks experience in production environments
* Strong experience building and operating data pipelines in production environments
* Advanced knowledge of **Python** and **SQL**
* Experience with distributed data processing and large-scale datasets
* Solid understanding of data engineering principles, including orchestration, performance optimization, and data quality
* Experience with **dbt** and modern data modeling practices
* Ability to independently drive technical initiatives and contribute to architectural decisions
* Professional proficiency in English


Nice-to-have skills:

* Experience with real-time data processing
* Experience migrating legacy data warehouse environments to cloud platforms
* Understanding of CI/CD, testing, monitoring, and platform governance within data ecosystems
* Understanding of Swedish

***This is your role***

* Design, develop, and maintain data pipelines on the Databricks platform
* Build and optimize scalable data integration and transformation solutions
* Work directly with Databricks notebooks, jobs, clusters, and workflows in production
* Develop reliable and performant solutions for processing large volumes of data
* Implement modern data modeling approaches using dbt
* Ensure high standards for data quality, traceability, scalability, and performance
* Contribute to the architecture and evolution of the organization's Azure-based data platform
* Establish and improve engineering best practices related to Databricks, CI/CD, testing, monitoring, and platform operations
* Collaborate closely with analysts, BI teams, and other stakeholders to deliver business-critical data solutions
* Support the migration from legacy data warehouse solutions to a modern Databricks-centric architecture

 ***What awaits you at Avenga?***

*At Avenga, everyone matters. We provide equal opportunities in recruitment, career development, and leadership, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age, religion, or any other characteristic. We are committed to fostering a work environment where our diverse community of employees, candidates, and business partners actively shapes our growth. By bringing together people from different backgrounds and experiences, we build a workplace where everyone feels free to be themselves while honoring the boundaries of others.*
company_description: Avenga is an international consultancy and technology solutions partner with 6000+ specialists globally.

title: UX Lead
description:
**Ska-krav:**  

* Du har arbetat som UX Designer i 5 år eller mer med att utforma appar, hemsidor, produkter och tjänster som balanserar användarbehov, affärsmål och tekniska begränsningar. Du har erfarenhet av en lead-roll.
* Du kan planera, samordna och prioritera UX-aktiviteter inom de olika tvärfunktionella teamen tillsammans med ansvarige UX. Ditt viktigaste verktyg är UX-processen, hela vägen från research till test.
* Du har erfarenhet av att vara i ledarroll där du stöttar och coachar flera designers i deras dagliga arbete och lyfter blicken strategiskt.
* Du kan driva frågor framåt och guida en grupp med olika kompetenser till designbeslut. Du trivs med att leda workshops och möten samt att presentera insikter och koncept.
* Du trivs med att bidra med UX hands-on men samtidigt ta strategiskt fokus vid behov.
* Du förstår affärsnyttan i design och kan argumentera för hur designförslag bidrar positivt för användare och företaget.
* Du har ett analytiskt sinne och är en stark problemlösare. Du är van att prata med utvecklare och förstår tekniska möjligheter.
* Din positiva attityd och goda kommunikationsförmåga gör dig till en uppskattad ledare. Du har ett stort intresse för hela kundupplevelsen med förståelse för helheten.
* Du håller ett öga på trender och utmanar konventioner där du ser att det är rätt.

**Du har erfarenhet av:**  

  

* Design thinking
* Informationsarkitektur
* Interaktionsflöden
* Interaktionsmönster
* Designsystem
* Tillgänglighet enligt WCAG 2.1 AA
* User research
* Agila utvecklingsmetoder/SAFe
 **Var god förklara hur du uppfyller alla kraven.**  

  

**Ort:** Stockholm  

**Period**: 01-07-2026-30-06-2027  

**Sista ansökningdag:** 10-06-2026  

  

*Vi presenterar löpande. Om du är intresserad av ett uppdrag rekommenderar vi att du skickar in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.*

title: Creative Designer
description:
**About Air Apps**
 At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.
   

  

 Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.
   

  

 Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.
   

  

**The Role**
 As a Creative Designer at Air Apps, you will play a crucial role in shaping our brand identity and delivering visually engaging designs across multiple platforms. You will collaborate with various teams to ensure a cohesive and compelling user experience, working on digital ads, social media content, landing pages, email campaigns, and print materials.
   

  

* This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


**Responsibilities**
* Develop and maintain a consistent visual identity that aligns with Air Apps’ brand guidelines.
* Create engaging graphics, illustrations, infographics, and layouts for web, social media, email campaigns, and print materials.
* Collaborate with Marketing, Product, and Content teams to translate requirements into compelling visual solutions.
* Iterate on designs based on feedback from stakeholders and refine assets using user insights and performance metrics.
* Ensure high-quality output and conduct design quality assurance across various channels.
* Utilize industry-standard design software (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite, Figma) to create and deliver assets in multiple formats.


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's degree in Graphic Design, Fine Arts, or a related field.
* Around 3 years of experience creating assets for digital marketing campaigns, social media, and web.
* Strong portfolio showcasing a range of design projects and styles.
* Knowledge of typography, color theory, layout, and design best practices.
* Proficiency in industry-standard design tools such as Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and Figma.
* Strong communication, time-management, and attention-to-detail skills.
* Ability to work in a fast-paced, remote environment and collaborate effectively across teams.


**What benefits are we offering?**
* Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
* Annual Bonus
* Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
* Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
* Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
* Childcare support.
* Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
* Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
* Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
* Meals 100% free at the hub.


**Diversity & Inclusion**
 At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.
   

  

**Application Disclaimer**
 At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.
   

  

 Compensation Range: €44K - €55K

title: Product Designer
description:
You know that bit where every company says they’re
 *“changing the world”* 
 ? Yeah, it can feel a little forced. But we like to think our story actually has some realness to it.
 



  





 Our story begins with
 **Shadi Bitar** 
 and
 **Ninos Malki** 
 , who came to Sweden from Syria as kids and discovered that books could be a bridge - helping them learn the language, understand the culture, and explore new worlds.
 



  





 A thought emerged,
 *what if everyone could have that?* 
 And just like that, Nextory was born - your all-access pass to read, listen, and discover new perspectives without limits.
 



 And now, we're on a quest to find a
 **Product Designer.** 




  





**Let us tell you about the role 📖** 




 As a Product Designer at Nextory, you will own the end-to-end design process, from discovery and validation to polished user experiences and visual execution. Working closely with Product Managers, Engineers, Analysts, and other stakeholders, you will help shape the future of our digital products through a combination of user insight, business understanding, and design excellence.
 



  





 Furthermore, you will
 


* Design intuitive end-to-end experiences that optimize customer journeys
* Craft high-quality UX and UI that balance customer needs, business goals, and brand experience.
* Create high-quality visual designs that elevate the customer experience beyond functionality while strengthening the Nextory brand.
* Drive the adoption of AI-powered and agentic workflows while leveraging data and experimentation to inform design decisions
 **.**



  





**Your story so far 👀** 




 We believe you thrive in environments where you can combine strategic thinking with hands-on execution and enjoy taking ownership of the entire design process. You enjoy working closely with cross-functional teams and are comfortable balancing user needs, business objectives, and technical constraints.
 



 We also believe that you have:
 


* Several years of experience as a Product Designer, UX/UI Designer, or similar role within a product-led technology company.
* Proven ability to own the full design process, from discovery to delivery.
* Proficiency in Figma, data-informed design, and using qualitative and quantitative insights to drive decisions.
* Experience working with AI-powered tools and agentic ways of working.



  





**The Nextory way of working 🏠** 




 At Nextory, we believe a strong and inclusive culture is key to our success and that culture flourishes when we meet and collaborate in person as much as possible. That’s why we spend the majority of the working time at our beautiful office!
 



  





 We are based in Stockholm, Sweden, with offices in Paris and Bangalore. Our hybrid work policy includes four days in the office and Wednesdays as a work-from-home day.
 



  





**Your next story starts here 💫** 




 We look forward to your application 💫. Applications are reviewed continuously and if you have any questions about the role, please contact
 
 erika.thorslund@nextory.com
 




  





*Please note that we do not offer relocation and that a six-month probationary period applies.*

title: Product Designer
description:
**Competencies**
 We're not optimizing for a specific background, we're optimizing for product sense.
   

  

 Here's what that looks like:
   

  

* You think in systems and flows, not just individual interfaces
* You are comfortable with complexity and know how to reduce it without oversimplifying
* You care deeply about usability, but also about strategy and outcomes
* You can collaborate fluently with engineers and product leads
* You are curious, opinionated, and willing to challenge assumptions
* You have strong UI craft and can design pixel-perfect, information-dense interfaces that stay clear, scalable, and easy to navigate
* You want real ownership, not just tickets in a backlog


 Experience with enterprise software, data-heavy products, or technical users is a plus, but raw ability, judgment, and drive matter more than pedigree.
   

  

**Trajectory**
 Planhat has spent a decade building an enterprise-grade platform for commercial teams. We integrate data, workflows, automation, AI, and collaboration into a single system that runs some of the most critical processes inside high-growth Fortune 500 companies. We're not building surface-level UI or feature-by-feature solutions. We've built a horizontal platform made up of composable primitives, and the same goes for our design approach.
   

  

 As a Product Designer at Planhat, you'll work on some of the most complex product challenges in B2B software:
   

  

* Design core platform experiences used daily by operators, leaders, and technical teams
* Turn abstract capabilities like data, automations, AI, and permissions into clear, usable workflows
* Collaborate deeply with product managers and engineers from problem framing to shipped outcome
* Build and evolve our design system: reusable components, patterns, and interaction foundations that keep the product consistent, scalable, and fast to ship
* Balance power and simplicity without dumbing things down


 You'll work close to the heart of the product, where decisions compound over years, not sprints.
   

  

**Definition of Success**
 Every week that we don't make the platform clearer and more intuitive is a missed opportunity for customers. We've built the engine. Now we're focused on how it feels to use.
   

  

* You are continuously raising the bar on UX foundations, delivering clearer patterns and experiences that make platform complexity feel simple and trusted
* You are consistently shipping design work that drives real adoption and measurable business impact, not just polished screens
* You are actively contributing to and evolving the design system, building reusable components and patterns that scale across the product
* You are growing into a design leader who shapes not just individual features but the overall product direction
* You are seen as a trusted collaborator by both engineers and product managers, working at the intersection of craft, strategy, and outcomes

title: Senior Product Designer
description:
We’re looking for a Senior product designer to help shape the next layer of SeenThis: a product where creative and marketing teams can build, run, measure, and improve high-performing video campaigns across channels.
   

  

 This is a role for an AI-native designer who likes complex problems, expert users, and fast execution. You’ll work close to product and engineering, own meaningful parts of our media and advertising product suite, and help turn complicated workflows into tools people want to use every day.
   

  

**About SeenThis**
 The open web has massive untapped advertising potential, but video has historically underperformed here. SeenThis is changing that. As a video advertising partner, we prove the open web delivers exceptional results, capturing more attention with cost-effective video that keeps publishers profitable and the internet independent. Since 2017, we've served billions of streams for 5,000+ brands across 50+ countries. We believe brands deserve reach wherever audiences are, publishers deserve sustainable revenue, and the internet deserves to stay open.
   

  

 Our team of 130+ employees works globally from offices in Stockholm, Oslo, London, Paris, Singapore, Hong Kong, New York, and Sydney. We're expanding across all teams and constantly seeking new colleagues who want to make real impact in a fast-moving, collaborative environment where innovation happens daily.
   

  

 Do you want to be part of our journey, where we dare to discover, own it, and win together?
   

  

 Join SeenThis!
   

  

**About The Role**
 Our adaptive streaming technology fixed the delivery problem for video advertising. Now we’re building the product layer on top.
   

  

 You’ll own parts of our media and advertising product suite, working closely with engineers, a PM, and the Design Lead to take ideas from first sketch to shipped product. You’ll help define how our product works, how it feels, and how product design works at SeenThis as the function continues to grow.
   

  

 While you will iterate and build fast, your role starts with discovery. You’ll be expected to become a domain expert for your part of our products. That means continually talking with customers, mapping their workflows, understanding the business context, and finding real problems.
   

  

 You’ll be expected to figure out what’s worth building before any screen is designed. Sometimes that means proper research or session data. Other times, a client call and a rough prototype may be the answer. That’s up to you.
   

  

 Getting this right is most of the job, because the wrong thing built well is still the wrong thing.
   

  

 This is also an execution-first and AI-native role. You’ll move quickly from problem to prototype to shipped product, using tools like Claude Code to explore ideas, test flows, and iterate faster.
   

  

 Building is cheap now; judgment is the scarce part. When AI can produce the screen, the work that matters is deciding what to build, what to cut, where to simplify, and when to push back. That part is still yours.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Run discovery for your product area: talk to customers, dig into the problem, map workflows, and decide what’s worth building before committing to a build.
* Become a domain expert for your part of the product, with a strong understanding of users, workflows, constraints, and business goals.
* Choose the right tool for each question: proper research when it warrants it, session data or support insights when that’s enough, and a fast prototype when that answers it sooner.
* Own user flows, interactions, and interfaces from first discovery through to shipped product.
* Design for expert users doing complex work, making powerful workflows feel clear, fast, and simple enough to use every day.
* Talk to users directly through client calls, support tickets, and session data, and form product opinions from what you find.
* Work hands-on with engineers through the build, not just at handoff.
* Use AI tools, including Claude Code, to design, prototype, and iterate. Build your own small tools when that’s faster.
* Contribute to product strategy and roadmap with your PM and the rest of the team.
* Help shape our design system and how design works at SeenThis while both are still taking shape.


**Qualifications**
* Have 5+ years of product design experience, with the seniority to own complex work from discovery through shipped product.
* Start with the problem, not the screen, and have shipped work where discovery shaped the outcome.
* Have designed complex digital products end to end, with work you can walk through and explain.
* Have a portfolio that shows clear thinking, polished UI, and real outcomes rather than well-composed screens.
* Move fluidly between early discovery, rough prototypes, high-fidelity execution, and hands-on iteration with engineers.
* Treat prototyping as a way to think, test, and align, not just a deliverable to produce.
* Already work AI-native, with real opinions about where Claude Code or similar tools help and where they get in the way.
* Are a designer who builds, not one who hands off.
* Are solid across UX, interaction, IA, and visual design, with the technical curiosity to understand how the product behaves, not only how it looks.
* Bring high ownership and low ego, and can drive your own work forward while the team and product are still forming.


**Application**
 As we review applications and interview on a continuous basis, please apply as soon as possible. Due to the high volume of applications received, we are unable to provide personalized feedback prior to the interview stage. Thank you for understanding!
   

  

 We conduct background checks on all SeenThis-hires to comply with our customers and protect business-critical information.
   

  

 We look forward to welcoming you to SeenThis!
   

  

 Status Quo-what? With courage and curiosity, we explore the unknown in the quest for game-changing-impact.
   

  

 To us, ownership is more than just another buzzword. It's our driving force. We own our projects and nurture them from idea to reality.
   

  

 To us, it's essential to forge deep connections both within SeenThis and with our clients. And, we approach every challenge and celebrate each success as a unified team
   

  

 The open web represents the internet's greatest untapped advertising opportunity. While 61% of consumer time happens here, across premium publishers, diverse voices, and independent content, only 29% of ad budgets follow. SeenThis is changing that.
   

  

 Our proprietary adaptive streaming technology proves the open web can outperform walled gardens, delivering 70% more attention and exceptional campaign results for brands while keeping publishers profitable and the internet independent.
   

  

 Since 2017, we've served over 40 billion impressions for 5,000+ brands across 50+ countries, partnering with agencies and publishers from eight offices worldwide.
   

  

 Brands deserve reach wherever their audiences are, publishers deserve sustainable revenue, and the internet deserves to stay open and thriving. SeenThis unlocks the open web's full potential. For more information, visit www.seenthis.co

title: Designer (Digital Experience & DTC) - Barebells
description:
*At* 
***Vitamin Well Group,***
*we provide great-tasting drinks, snacks, and protein products. Our brands Vitamin Well, NOCCO, and Barebells are available across most of Europe, North America, the MENA region, and parts of Asia Pacific. With a revenue of 650 million euros, we operate in over 40 markets, with headquarters in Stockholm and offices in fourteen countries around the world.*
**About The Role**
 As
 **Designer (Digital Experience & DTC)** 
 , you will design and elevate the customer journey for
 **Barebells** 
 across our digital ecosystem. You will work across DTC websites, marketplaces such as Amazon, and CRM touchpoints, ensuring that every interaction is intuitive, engaging, and aligned with the brand.
   

  

 You will translate brand strategy into high-performing digital experiences, combining strong design craft with a structured, data-informed approach. Working closely with e-commerce, growth, CRM, and brand teams, you will identify opportunities to improve the customer journey and continuously refine how our digital channels perform.
   

  

 You will play a key role in ensuring consistency and quality across all digital touchpoints, while contributing to improvements in conversion, retention, and overall user experience through thoughtful and well-executed design.
   

  

 This role is based at our headquarters in Stockholm and reports to the Global Brand Manager Barebells.
   

  

 In short, you will:
   

  

* Design end-to-end customer journeys across DTC, marketplaces, and CRM channels
* Translate brand strategy into consistent and engaging digital experiences across markets
* Apply UX and UI best practices to improve usability, clarity, and flow
* Use data, insights, and testing to inform design decisions and iterate on solutions
* Contribute to improving conversion, retention, and overall customer experience through design
* Leverage AI tools to support workflows, exploration, and personalisation
* Collaborate closely with e-commerce, growth, CRM, and brand teams


 What you can expect
   

  

* Real impact on how our DTC and marketplace experiences perform
* Short decision paths and the ability to test and implement quickly
* Close work with growth, e-commerce, and brand teams across markets
* A high pace where ideas are tested, not parked
* The opportunity to push how AI is used within design and customer experience


 Who are you
   

  

 You are a designer with a strong foundation in UX and UI, and a clear interest in how digital experiences perform. You care about both how things look and how they work, and you are comfortable using data and insights to inform your decisions.
   

  

 You enjoy working across the full customer journey and collaborating with different functions, bringing a structured and thoughtful approach to improving digital experiences over time.
   

  

**Essential Skills To Succeed**
* Minimum of 6 years’ experience in UX/UI design within DTC, e-commerce, or other conversion-driven digital environments
* Strong understanding of UX, UI, and customer journey design
* Ability to use data, insights, and testing to inform and improve design decisions
* Experience working with modern design tools and workflows
* Familiarity with AI tools within design processes
* Ability to collaborate across teams and stakeholders
* Experience working across multiple markets or brands is an advantage


 Apply by June 28th 2026.
   

  

**We review applications continuously and may close the role early.
   

  

 Please include your CV and portfolio — we want to see what you have built and how you think.**
**Life at Vitamin Well Group**
 Our growing family is made up of over 600 employees, representing a variety of cultures, experiences and diverse backgrounds. This diversity is a great asset as we continue our expansion and evolve our portfolio. At the same time, Vitamin Well is still very much a Swedish company, promoting Swedish values when it comes to both business and people. We treat each other in a fair and equal way, and we respect one another based on our qualities. As we expand Vitamin Well Group’s business, we are also committed to minimizing our environmental impact and continuously strive to lower emissions from for example production, logistics, and packaging.
   

  

 Working at Vitamin Well, we provide a dynamic environment defined by ambition, dedication, and an entrepreneurial spirit. In our action-oriented culture, the ability to make swift decisions is paramount in bringing ideas to fruition. We are committed to fostering an atmosphere where every individual's unique strengths contribute to our collective success. As a health and exercise-oriented company, we inspire an active lifestyle by partnering with top-performing athletes, but we also promote and support exercise among our employees.
   

  

*As part of the recruitment process, we may ask you to conduct a personality test as well as a test that measures logical ability. The purpose of the tests is to evaluate candidates as objective as possible and identify the best possible match for the role. The tests are based on solid research and are taken through our partner Alva Labs.*

title: Frontend Engineer - Music
description:
The Rights Systems team builds and operates Spotify Rights Center (SRC), Spotify’s rights management platform that enables the company and its partners to identify, manage, and enforce music licensing rights across creator- and artist-generated content.
   

  

 Comparable in scope to YouTube’s Content ID, SRC is designed specifically for Spotify’s ecosystem and plays an important role in supporting new formats like video and other emerging content types. Over the past year the team launched SRC from inception to a fully operational production platform, now live and used by real rightsholders.
   

  

 The platform includes automated content scanning, policy management, enforcement pipelines, dispute workflows, and analytics. The team has grown from a single squad to three squads (approximately 29 engineers, designers, and product specialists) and continues to scale as a strategic investment area for Spotify over the next several years.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Build and evolve the Spotify Rights Center web application used by music labels, publishers, and other rights holders to manage how their content appears on Spotify
* Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver intuitive and high-quality user experiences
* Develop complex workflows that support large datasets, territory-level permissions, and multi-step processes for managing rights and disputes
* Integrate frontend systems with multiple backend services to support scalable and reliable data flows
* Write performant, accessible, and well-tested frontend code using modern development practices
* Partner with backend engineers to design and evolve APIs that support the needs of the product
* Contribute to product discovery and ideation with engineers, researchers, and product specialists
* Help improve engineering practices and advocate for strong frontend architecture and quality standards
* Support a collaborative team environment that encourages learning, experimentation, and growth


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have experience building modern web applications using TypeScript and React or a similar framework
* You are comfortable managing complex application state and data flows
* You have experience integrating frontend systems with APIs such as REST, gRPC, or GraphQL
* You understand how to design responsive interfaces that handle loading states, pagination, streaming data, and error handling gracefully
* You care deeply about creating intuitive and accessible user experiences
* You have experience building enterprise or B2B applications with complex workflows, permissions, and large datasets
* You write high-quality code and validate it using modern testing approaches such as unit, integration, or end-to-end testing
* You take ownership of solutions from requirements through deployment and iteration based on user feedback
* You enjoy learning new domains and problem spaces and collaborating with teammates to solve meaningful problems


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice
   

  

 Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Kravanalytiker
description:
Vi har nu ett nytt behov och söker därför en konsult och kollega med nedan kvalifikationer. Tjänsten är en del av vår konsultverksamhet vilket innebär att du blir anställd av oss och arbetar hos kund alt. interna projekt och åtaganden.
   

  

**Din profil (Skall krav)**
* Minst 6 års relevant och aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet av arbete som kravanalytiker i en större organisation med minst 1000 anställda.
* Relevant och aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet av att arbeta enligt en agil kravmetodik.
* Relevant och aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet av samarbete i uppdrag med roller som arkitekter, UX och utvecklingsteam.
* Relevant och aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet av att identifiera, analysera, dokumentera och följa upp krav.


**Meriterande**
* Relevant och aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet av att arbeta som kravanalytiker i regelstyrda och komplexa miljöer
* Relevant och aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet av arbete som kravanalytiker inom en myndighet.
* Erfarenhet av arbete inom SAFe ramverket.


**Sista ansökningdatum:** 
 10-06-2026
   

  

**Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**
 🚀 Spännande uppdrag hos våra många kunder. Vi fokuserar på långsiktiga uppdrag, vilket ger dig möjlighet att skapa värde för kunden samtidigt som du utvecklas själv.
   

  

 🌟 En trygg månadslön – tillsammans bestämmer vi en grundlön baserad på dina uppdrag.
   

  

 💼 Tjänstepension och stora möjligheter till löneväxling.
   

  

 🌴 Semester - Önskar du mer än 30 dagar semester, kanske 60 dagar?
   

  

 🏥 Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring, olycksfallsförsäkring och livförsäkring för ökad trygghet.
   

  

 📚 Kompetensutveckling – du får själv sätta din budget och välja vilka utbildningar du vill delta i.
   

  

 🕑 Livskvalitet - Har du kanske svårt att få ihop pusslet i vardagen och önskar arbeta lite mindre och ändå kunna tjäna lika mycket om inte mer?
   

  

* Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande, med hänsyn till höga arbetstrycket är det extra viktigt med en kort motivering för tjänsten som förklarar hur man möter kraven.

title: Senior Frontend Engineer (Product Experience)
description:
**About Us**
 Soundtrack is a B2B scale-up company providing music streaming services for businesses. We serve small customers like the café around the corner, and much bigger brands like Joe & the Juice, Toni & Guy and TAG Heuer. On the inside, we're a bunch of talented, motivated and humble designers, engineers and music experts. We believe in product-led growth, where the product is the primary driver of customer acquisition, conversion and expansion.
   

  

**The Role**
 The Product Experience team is a multidisciplinary team responsible for our user facing touch points - mobile, desktop, and web apps. As a senior frontend engineer in the team, you’ll be working on a wide range of user-centric projects together with other engineers, product owners, UI/UX designers, researchers, music experts and analysts.
   

  

 Your primary focus will be to work on new features and continuous improvements for our apps built on React/React Native (written in TypeScript). As a team we continuously iterate based on insights, A/B testing and qualitative user feedback.
   

  

 You can check out the How it works page to see what kind of features you will be working on or download our app and sign up for a free trial to get a first hand experience.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Building, releasing and continuously improving existing features together with UI/UX designers, researchers and analysts.
* Developing and maintaining consistent and reusable UI components/libraries used by multiple teams
* Contribute to the team’s vision, strategy and agenda
* Helping the team grow as a whole by sharing knowledge and lifting each other up


**About You**
 We're always looking to work with people who are friendly, professional, humble, open, and with a passion for details. You are probably not a stranger to describing yourself as:
   

  

* Self-sustaining – you can work independently, taking initiatives and planning your own time
* Curious – you enjoy learning new things to grow both professionally as well as on a personal level
* User-focused – you have a strong intuition for great user experiences and enjoy sweating the details that make products feel polished
* A good communicator – you enjoy discussing different solutions and strategies for technical challenges
* Agile – you feel comfortable in a dynamic environment where focus lies in what’s important here and now, with the ability to realize when it's time to change the game plan and when to stick to it
* Humble – you are an open person, you say what you mean and mean what you say
* Street smart – crafty, able to build solutions with limited resources
* Not a perfectionist – but disciplined and methodical in your approach to solving problems


**Mandatory Requirements**
* 5+ years of professional experience in building robust and maintainable web/mobile apps
* Strong proficiency in TypeScript/JavaScript
* Worked with a JS framework such as React, React Native, Angular or Vue.js
* Experienced with Git or another distributed source management system


**Beneficial Requirements (not Required)**
* General interest in music and/or music technology
* iOS, Android, Mac, and/or Windows (Electron) app development
* Backend development (our backend is primarily built in Go, Scala and python)
* Container based deployments using Docker/Kubernetes
* Modern web development
* Strong experience with React/React Native
* GraphQL APIs and libraries (we use Apollo)
* State management (we use Redux Toolkit)
* Automated testing (especially front-end)
* Front-end development/build pipelines using Vite/Webpack/Rollup or similar
* While this is a hybrid role, we prefer candidates who live somewhat close to Stockholm for occasional team days at our office


**Employee Benefits**
* We offer a flexible workplace with the possibility to work remotely
* Generous pension plan
* Yearly budget for health, wellness and personal development
* Laptop and mobile phone of your choice with included cellular subscription
* Regular social activities such as team events, meet ups, after works and off-sites
* Daily breakfast served at the office


 At Soundtrack, we believe that diversity fuels creativity and innovation. We're committed to building a team that reflects a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We welcome applications from individuals of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and identities.
   

  

 We reserve the right to close this vacancy early if we identify a suitable candidate before the application deadline. To ensure consideration, we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.
   

  

 Please note that we only accept applications submitted via our career page and do not accept applications by e-mail.

title: Manual QA Engineer (Core systems)
description:
**About Ubiquiti**
 At Ubiquiti Inc., we create technology platforms for Businesses, Smart Homes, and Internet Service Providers, driven by our goal to connect everyone, everywhere. To date, Ubiquiti has shipped over 100 million devices worldwide, from ISP networking products to next generation of IT solutions. Our growth is made possible by the dedicated team of hundreds behind the scenes. From software developers and product managers to designers and strategists, Team UI is driven to achieve our common goal: Rethinking IT. At Ubiquiti, you’ll heighten your potential and broaden your horizons - all while shaping the future of connectivity.
   

  

**Join forces with us on our mission to build a better IT industry. We are looking for a skilled Manual QA Engineer (Core systems) for our team in Stockholm, Sweden.**
***Please note that applicants must live in Sweden and hold a valid work permit at the time of application to be considered for this role.***
**Responsibilities**
* Plan and lead manual testing initiatives to ensure software releases meet or exceed requirements
* Perform manual testing of both UX/UI and functionality to ensure product requirements are met or exceeded
* Work with networking equipment to achieve adequate test coverage
* Analyze project documentation, release notes, and other project artifacts to identify new test scenarios or update existing ones
* Conduct ad-hoc and scripted manual tests
* Report bugs/issues to development teams and assist with troubleshooting


**Requirements**
* 3+ years of experience testing software applications, web applications and mobile applications
* Experience testing hardware/IoT devices
* General understanding of networking concepts (Internet, WiFi, VLAN, Firewall, VPN, etc.)
* Thorough understanding of QA methodologies and best practices
* Passion for delivering an excellent user experience and product quality
* Experience using modern AI tools such as Claude, ChatGPT, Codex, Cursor, or similar
* Excellent communication skills and high proficiency in written and spoken English


**Nice To Have**
* Experience leveraging AI/LLMs to improve engineering productivity, testing efficiency, documentation quality, or other measurable outcomes
* Experience with web test automation
* Experience with scripting languages (e.g., JavaScript/TypeScript)
* Experience with CI/CD tools (e.g., Jenkins, AWS CodePipeline)


**Benefits**
* Competitive compensation
* Occupational pension, health insurance, private health insurance, life insurance and work-injury insurance, and wellness allowance (5000 SEK)
* Flexible work hours and a hybrid work model
* Access to all the latest Ubiquiti products for home use

title: Python Backend Engineer (Transcripts)
description:
Quartr is a software company serving the world of finance. Every day, leading companies and financial institutions worldwide use our products to research faster and understand deeper.
  
For too long, markets have been obsessed with numbers. Everyone has access to the same quantitative data, while qualitative insights – the words, the signals, the nuance – have remained mostly in the shadows. We make those insights accessible, searchable, and actionable.
  
Our products are used daily by some of the most sophisticated teams in finance and tech, including major hedge funds and Magnificent 7 companies, and over a million finance professionals have downloaded our mobile app.
  
We're backed by some of the smartest minds in our industry and growing fast at a global scale, and we're still at the very beginning of what we expect to be a multi-decade journey.
  
We are looking for a Python Backend Engineer to play a key role in scaling and improving our transcripts pipelines. If you love engineering robust software, extracting information from text, and thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, we'd love to hear from you!
  
Our transcripts team builds and maintains the pipelines that power one of Quartr's most important data assets: transcripts. Your work will provide the core data foundation that powers essential product features and drives future product development.
  
What you'll do
  
Build scalable systems for transcript ingestion, processing, and enrichment from diverse text sources
  
  

Optimise backend performance for high-volume, text-heavy workloads and parallel processing
  
  

**Work on text processing and information extraction:** entity recognition, metadata enrichment, and semantic structuring of transcripts
  
  

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to shape features from concept to release, ensuring outstanding user experiences
  
  

Monitor and troubleshoot data pipelines to ensure smooth operation and timely data delivery
  
  

Who you are
  
Self-driven, proactive, and confident in taking full ownership of projects
  
  

A keen interest in NLP, speech-to-text, or large-scale text pipelines is advantageous
  
  

Comfortable learning new technologies and eager to improve products and processes
  
  

Balance pragmatism and craftsmanship, knowing when to move quickly and when to build for long-term scalability - even in complex systems with many interdependent components
  
  

Communicate clearly and effectively, thriving in a high-paced, collaborative environment
  
  

Based in Sweden with a valid work permit
  
  

Required skills
  
3+ years of professional experience building robust backends in Python, preferably for large-scale or data-intensive systems
  
  

Proven experience developing and maintaining REST APIs using frameworks such as FastAPI, Flask, or Django
  
  

Familiarity with asynchronous programming and distributed processing
  
  

Ability to explain code and technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  
  

Ability to propose durable, simple solutions to complex problems
  
  

What we offer
  
**Do your life's work:** The world's leading finance professionals use Quartr daily. We do fewer things better and hold a very high bar for quality.
  
  

**Join the foundational team:** We're still at the very beginning of a multi-decade journey. You'll have a big influence on product, culture, and how we win.
  
  

**Meritocracy over hierarchy:** You'll join a culture where the best idea wins. We value clear thinking, leave ego at the door, and obsess over the details.
  
  

**Office culture with flexibility:** Join us in one of our offices. We value being together, while also offering the flexibility to work from home when needed.
  
  

**Compensation & well-being:** We offer competitive salaries, benefits, and a generous wellness allowance to support your physical and mental health.
  
  

We include occupational pension for all employees in Sweden.

title: Lösningsarkitekt PLM till NIBE AB
description:
Vill du vara med och utveckla framtidens PLM‑lösningar i en global industrikoncern? Vi söker nu en Lösningsarkitekt som vill bidra till att stärka och vidareutveckla våra system och arbetssätt.
  
Om rollen
  
Som Lösningsarkitekt tar du fram programlösningar för att implementera våra PLM‑initiativ, baserat på de arbetspaket som projekten definierar. Du arbetar obehindrat i Visual Studio, C++, C#, BMIDE Java och Eclipse.
  
Inledningsvis kommer du att stötta verksamheten genom att genomföra mindre förändringar och vidareutveckling av funktionalitet i vårt PLM‑system.
  
För rätt person finns goda möjligheter att vara med och påverka samt utveckla både rollen och arbetssättet inom PLM‑avdelningen.
  
Placering
  
NIBEs PLM‑team är placerat på huvudkontoret i Markaryd.
  
Din profil
  
Vi söker dig som är nyfiken, självgående och drivande, och som ser möjligheter att bidra till utvecklingen av vår PLM‑funktion.
  
Du har ett strukturerat arbetssätt och ett intresse för metodik som främjar informationssamordning. Samtidigt har du god samarbetsförmåga och en naturlig lagkänsla.
  
Lämplig bakgrund kan vara ingenjör, projektledare eller utbildning inom system- eller datavetenskap, gärna i kombination med erfarenhet som CAD‑konstruktör. Du har god förståelse för produktutvecklingsprocesser, livscykelhantering och kvalitetsarbete.
  
  

**Vi ser gärna att du har:**  

Erfarenhet av arbete i PLM‑system, gärna Siemens Teamcenter som är vårt arbetsverktyg
  
Kunskap i Autodesk Inventor, Solid Edge, SolidWorks, Infor XA och Microsoft Visio
  
Erfarenhet från tillverkande industri är meriterande
  
  

Om NIBE
  
Vi finns för att ge världen bättre energilösningar. Ända sedan 1952 har vi arbetat passionerat och målinriktat med att utveckla nya metoder för bättre energieffektivitet. På så sätt spelar vi en viktig roll i den globala omställningen till ett mer hållbart samhälle. Våra produkter förlitar sig på den finaste svenska ingenjörskonsten. Vi har en stark marknadsposition och gör stora satsningar för framtiden. Vår företagskultur är familjär och entreprenörsdriven. Den bygger på lång tradition och en rik historia där ledorden är enkelhet, ödmjukhet och pålitlighet. Vi ser långsiktigt på din anställning och erbjuder dig en arbetsplats med goda karriärmöjligheter. Tillsammans ser vi till att du får utlopp för din talang, kreativitet och kunskap.
  
NIBE i siffror
  
NIBE AB är en modern och framgångsrik industri med en omsättning på drygt 5 miljarder kr/år med drygt 1250 anställda i Markaryd. NIBE tillhör de marknadsledande i Europa med ett produktsortiment som består av marknadens bredaste program av värmepumpar, varmvattenberedare, solpaneler, biobränsleprodukter, ventilationsprodukter, fjärrvärmeutrustning, braskaminer mm.
  
NIBE AB ingår i det börsnoterade NIBE Industrier som har en omsättning på cirka 40 miljarder kr och mer än 21 000 anställda i över 30 länder.
  
Välkommen att söka tjänsten
  
Låter det som något för dig? Ansök via vår hemsida http://www.nibe.se under ”Lediga tjänster”. Vi rekryterar löpande, så skicka gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Sista ansökningsdag är 22 juni, men urval och intervjuer sker kontinuerligt så vänta inte med att skicka in.
  
Har du frågor eller vill veta mer? Kontakta gärna någon av våra kontaktpersoner nedan.

title: .NET Software Engineer
description:
**Job Purpose:**  

A .NET Developer for Microsoft .net core environment will be responsible for design and development of back-
  
end mission critical part of the sportsbook trading system.
  
Candidate will be actively involved in all phases of software development life cycle; this includes requirements
  
gathering, researching, initial configuration, set up, design, documentation, testing, debugging, updates,
  
ongoing support, integration, maintenance and troubleshooting.
  
  

**Skills & Qualifications:**  

Strong analytical, conceptual, and problem-solving abilities with attention to detail
  
Ability to multi-task and manage multiple assignments in a fast-paced environment
  
Strong ability to analyse business requirements and build according to business specifications
  
Fluent in English
  
Initiative to work independently, but also able to work effectively with team members
  
Flexibility and adaptability to business requirements and priority changes
  
Experience of working in the sports betting industry or finance
  
  

**Knowledge & Experience:**  

Degree or Diploma in Computer Science, Engineering, or Information Systems.
  
2+ years of development using C#
  
Merit for
  
Experience of developing real-time systems
  
Experience in designing and developing scalable and high-performance distributed systems
  
Experience working on high-consequence production systems
  
Experience of working with Docker and Kubernetes
  
Experience with Redis
  
Experience of working in a CI / CD environment
  
Experience triaging production issues in complex systems
