 Systemlandskapet består av flera system kopplade till logistik, lager och inköp. Här kommer du att arbeta med både större och mindre lösningar, inklusive avancerade automationsflöden.
 



  





 Du kommer bland annat att:
 


* Leda och samordna projekt inom systemuppgraderingar, integrationer och förändringar
* Samla in, analysera och omsätta affärsbehov till krav mot IT och leverantörer
* Säkerställa att systemen uppfyller compliance- och säkerhetskrav
* Driva förbättringsinitiativ inom digitalisering och automatisering av supply chain-flöden
* Säkerställa att systemlösningar är integrerade och stödjer verksamhetens mål över tid
* Delta i leverantörsdialoger och bidra i både taktiska och strategiska forum



  





**Utbildning, erfarenhet och personliga egenskaper** 



* Relevant eftergymnasial utbildning inom IT, logistik eller supply chain
* Minst tre års erfarenhet av arbete med system kopplade till logistik, lager eller supply chain
* Erfarenhet av kravhantering och att översätta verksamhetsbehov till systemkrav
* Erfarenhet av att driva projekt eller förbättringsarbete
* Erfarenhet av IT-system kopplade till logistikprocesser och lagerstyrning som WMS, TMS och inköpssystem exempelvis Astro, Centiro, Relex , Pipechain eller Infor M3
* Flytande svenska och goda kunskaper i engelska



  





 För att passa i rollen tror vi att du arbetar strukturerat och har förmåga att driva arbete framåt även när flera initiativ pågår samtidigt. Du är bekväm i dialog med olika delar av verksamheten och kan hantera situationer där det finns flera perspektiv och prioriteringar.
 



  





 Du trivs med att ta ansvar för att sätta dig in i komplexa flöden och har tålamod att bygga förståelse över tid. Samtidigt har du ett eget driv och en vilja att hitta lösningar som fungerar i praktiken. Rollen kräver också att du kan vara neutral i diskussioner och driva projekt framåt även när det finns olika viljor.
 



  





**Övrig information** 




 Start: Enligt överenskommelse
 



 Plats: Bromma
 



 Lön: Enligt överenskommelse
 



  





 Vi använder en kompetensbaserad metodik i alla rekryteringsprocesser för att säkerställa fördomsfria urval. Vi jobbar också med löpande urval, vilket innebär att vi tar ner annonsen när tillräckligt många kandidater har ansökt. Om du blir aktuell för tjänsten kommer vi att kontakta dig för en första telefonintervju. Oavsett om du går vidare i processen eller inte så kommer du att få återkoppling på din ansökan.
 



  





 Har du frågor? Hör gärna av dig!
 



  





 📧 info@bravura.se
 



 📞 010-171 47 10
 



  





 Vi rekommenderar att du skickar in din ansökan omgående då vi gör ett löpande urval.
 



 Välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Pédopsychiatre (relocate to Ireland)
description:
We are looking for Consultant Psychiatrists specialized in child and adolescent (Pédopsychiatre) to work in Ireland.
 



  





 Posts are based within a multidisciplinary team environment and focuses on assessment, diagnosis, treatment, and ongoing care for individuals under 18 years of age.
 



  





**These are specific purpose contracts of 12 months initially.** 




**Salaries between €235,862 to €283,318 per year based on experience working 37 hours per week.** 




**30 days of annual leave, 10 days of public holidays and up to 10 days of medical education leave.** 




  





 Main Duties & Responsibilities
 



  




* Deliver specialist assessment, diagnostic formulation, and treatment of mental illnesses and/or mental disorders in children and adolescents, in line with the National Standardised Operating Procedure for CAMHS and subsequent national policies and guidelines.
* Ensure duties are carried out in a timely manner to minimise delays for patients and service disruption.
* Maintain accurate, detailed, and contemporaneous medical records of all clinical interactions.
* Provide prompt notification to the Executive Clinical Director and the Mental Health Commission of any serious untoward incidents, in accordance with relevant regulations.
* Work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team to provide a high standard of care.



  





 Eligibility Criteria
 



  





 Applicants must:
 


* Register as specialist in the Specialist Division of the Register of Medical Practitioners maintained by the Medical Council in Ireland (we will give full support to understand the process and apply for it).
* Specialist in
 **Child and Adolescent Psychiatry (Pédopsychiatre).**
* Experience as a specialist in child and adolescent psychiatry (minimum 3 months).
* Good english level
* Willing to relocate to Ireland

title: Corporate Governance Research Analyst
description:
Let’s be #BrilliantTogether
   

  

 What you will do
   

  

 ISS is looking for a talented and self-motivated research analyst to join our Nordics team in Stockholm, starting in July/August 2026. As a research analyst, you will be guided by our experienced analysts who will provide you with the training and practical skills needed to get off to a flying start.
   

  

 As a Corporate Governance Research Analyst, you will become an integral part of the team with the crucial role of helping deliver analysis, data, research, and vote recommendations to our institutional shareholder clients, such as hedge/mutual funds, pension funds and other large asset managers. The work will take place both during the “proxy voting season”, a high tempo period when the annual general meetings of most publicly traded companies in the Nordics take place. During this period, our role is to support our clients in making well-informed voting decisions on the topics covered by the AGM, for example equity issuances, M&As, board elections, and executive compensation.
   

  

 Outside the proxy season, you will do other analytical work and research, expanding expertise and supporting with optimizations and preparations for the next proxy season, alongside the continuous production of research reports for both annual and extraordinary general meetings.
   

  

 As part of the ISS team, you will, along with others, analyze governance structures and performance of publicly listed companies and provide investors with the appropriate vote recommendations. In addition, you will gather publicly available data from publicly listed companies.
   

  

 By leveraging analyst expertise on corporate governance, colored by constantly developing market trends, standards, client expectations and practices you and the team will prepare reports detailing the analyses and explaining why a particular vote recommendation was given.
   

  

 What you will bring to ISS
   

  

* Fluent in English (both spoken and written).
* A minimum one of the following languages to a native standard: Danish, Swedish, Norwegian, Finnish or Icelandic.
* A relevant bachelor's or master's degree in economics, Business, Finance, Law, or Political Sciences or in the process of obtaining such a degree.
* A prior experience in a relevant area will be considered as an advantage.
* A strong analytical ability, numeracy, strong oral and written communication skills.
* A strong reader with the ability to assimilate large quantities of information, work independently and take initiative while also being a team player.
* An organized and structured mind who can reason logically under given parameters.
* An interest in ESG, capital markets and business.


 But above all else we are most interested in the things that make you, you. The unique personal qualities, extracurricular activities and accomplishments that show who you are as a person and what value you could bring to the team.
   

  

**What You Will Gain From This Position**
* Develop a deeper understanding within the world of corporate governance and responsible investment.
* Receive in-depth training and specific skills to help fulfil your career aspirations.
* Expand your know-how and contribute significantly to client work that has real world impact.
* Make a difference and perform in a workplace of integrity and transparency.
* An ability to make nuanced, principled, and well-argued decisions based on a framework of rules and practices.
* Hands-on experience working with a global market leader.


 Considering the broad range of proposals put forward during general meetings, you will gain valuable experience working on matters related to a wide range of topics spanning the financial markets, ESG, corporate and EU law
   

  

**What You Can Expect From Us**
 At ISS STOXX, our people are our driving force. We are committed to building a culture that values diverse skills, perspectives, and experiences. We hire the best talent in our industry and empower them with the resources, support, and opportunities to grow—professionally and personally.
   

  

 Together, we foster an environment that fuels creativity, drives innovation, and shapes our future success.
   

  

 Let’s empower, collaborate, and inspire.
   

  

 Let’s be #BrilliantTogether.
   

  

**About ISS STOXX**
 ISS STOXX GmbH is a leading provider of research and technology solutions for the financial market. Established in 1985, we offer top-notch benchmark and custom indices globally, helping clients identify investment opportunities and manage portfolio risks. Our services cover corporate governance, sustainability, cyber risk, and fund intelligence. Majority-owned by Deutsche Börse Group, ISS STOXX has over 3,400 professionals in 33 locations worldwide, serving around 6,400 clients, including institutional investors and companies focused on ESG, cyber, and governance risk. Clients trust our expertise to make informed decisions for their stakeholders' benefit.
   

  

 Founded in 1985 as Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS) Inc., ISS Governance is a leading global provider of independent and objective shareholder meeting research and recommendations, providing multiple voting policy choices as well as end-to-end workflow solutions for institutional investors. More than 1,600 clients worldwide utilize ISS Governance’s actionable expertise to help them make informed investment stewardship decisions, and to help them manage their voting responsibilities. Covering over 50,000 meetings annually, ISS Governance leverages its extensive global footprint, deep experience, high quality data and analysis, unified client support, and technology infrastructure to continuously evolve and extend its innovative suite of solutions to meet clients’ evolving portfolio, fiduciary, and stewardship requirements.
   

  

 Visit our website: https://www.issgovernance.com
   

  

 View additional open roles: https://www.issgovernance.com/join-the-iss-team/
   

  

 The Swedish Confederation of Professional Associations (SACO) is a politically independent central organization for 22 unions comprising university graduates in Sweden. You are welcome to contact the local representatives of SACO at ISS-Ethix on SACO@iss-esg.com. However, note that no applications sent to this e-mail address will be accepted.
   

  

*Institutional Shareholder Services (“ISS”) is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity and inclusion. It is our policy to prohibit discrimination or harassment against any applicant or employee on the basis of race, color, ethnicity, creed, religion, sex, age, height, weight, citizenship status, national origin, social origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression, pregnancy status, marital status, familial status, mental or physical disability, veteran status, military service or status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law (referred to as “protected status”). All activities including, but not limited to, recruiting and hiring, recruitment advertising, promotions, performance appraisals, training, job assignments, compensation, demotions, transfers, terminations (including layoffs), benefits, and other terms, conditions, and privileges of employment, are and will be administered on a non-discriminatory basis, consistent with all applicable federal, state, and local requirements.*

title: Kundansvarig säljare till Framtiden
description:
Vi söker dig som drivs av att vinna! Din inställning och vilja är viktigare än erfarenhet. Vi värdesätter egenskaper som laganda, tävlingsinstinkt och förmågan att ge allt för att nå målet. Här får du möjlighet att ta din vinnarskalle till nya höjder i en inspirerande och utvecklande säljroll.
 



  





**DINA ARBETSUPPGIFTER** 




 Som kundansvarig säljare hos Framtiden får du en nyckelroll i att förena talanger med spännande karriärmöjligheter. Du är företagets professionella ansikte utåt och bygger långsiktiga relationer med våra kunder.
 



  





**Din vardag är varierad, med grunden nedan:** 



* Prospektera nya kunder
* Hantera kalla och varma samtal till kund
* Identifiera personalbehov hos nya och befintliga kunder
* Skräddarsy lösningar inom bemanning och rekrytering för kunden, agera rådgivande till kunds rekryteringsbehov
* Genomföra säljmöte, både digitalt och främst fysiskt på plats hos kund
* Uppföljning och kundvård på både kort och lång sikt



  





**DIN PROFIL** 




 Vi söker dig som är hungrig att utvecklas och har drivet att lära dig mer inom försäljning! Du är målmedveten och resultatorienterad. Du trivs att arbeta med frihet under ansvar, och driver själv på för dig egen utveckling! Du tar ägande över ditt arbete och driver gärna på din omgivning för att lyckas tillsammans!
 



  





**Krav:** 



* Minst 2 års erfarenhet inom försäljning
* Flytande i svenska och engelska
* B-körkort



  





**Meriterande:** 



* Erfarenhet av uppsökande försäljning, ex telefon- eller fältförsäljning
* B2B-erfarenhet
* Erfarenhet inom IT/ Teknik



  





**OM FRAMTIDEN** 




 Framtiden arbetar med både bemanning och rekrytering, vi vill göra skillnad i människors liv. Vi gör skillnad genom att hjälpa människor att hitta rätt jobb och rätt kollega. Vi är specialister på att rekrytera talanger till rätt företag främst inom IT/Teknik, ekonomi och kontor. Framtiden startade 2004 och finns idag på sju orter i Sverige.
 



  





 Som medarbetare på Framtiden skall det vara roligt att gå till jobbet! Vi är ett ambitiöst och härligt gäng som gillar att umgås med varandra och samtidigt utvecklas. Här strävar vi alltid efter att bli vårt bästa jag genom utbildning, träning och ständig utveckling i våra roller.
 



  





 För oss på Framtiden är balansen mellan jobb och privatliv viktigt, därför tillämpar vi förtroendetid och flexibel arbetsplats. Vi har också förmåner som exempelvis utbildningsbidrag, väl tilltaget friskvårdsbidrag och gemensam frukost varje dag. Det finns möjlighet till delägarskap. Vi erbjuder attraktiv lön och bonusmodell.
 



  





 Vi står för hög service, kvalitet och har ett klimat som är familjärt och grundat i sunda värderingar. Om du vill ha en arbetsplats präglad av en positiv kultur där vi, medarbetarna, är i fokus är Framtiden rätt ställe för dig.
 



  





**VÅR REKRYTERINGSPROCESS** 




 Vår rekryteringsprocess följer nedan:
 


* Telefonintervju
* Personlighets- och logiktest
* Caseövning
* Intervju med rekryterare
* Referenstagning
* Intervju med rekryterande chef



  





**VILLKOR** 




 Start: Augusti
 



 Omfattning: 100%
 



 Ort: Stockholm
 



  





 Vi arbetar löpande med urval och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdatum, vänta därför inte med att skicka iväg din ansökan. Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!

title: Strategic Sourcing Manager
description:
*Är du en driven inköpare som vill vara med på Bankgirots viktiga uppdrag?*
**Välkommen till Bankgirot!**
 Vi på Bankgirot har gjort ett aktivt val att ta ansvar för något större än oss själva: Sveriges betalsystem. Sedan 1959 har våra produkter påverkat miljoner människors liv varje dag. Vi är i en omvandling och söker dig som vill vara med och forma vår framtid. Oavsett roll delar du vår drivkraft: att skapa något som består, som gör skillnad, och som andra kan lita på. Du vill verka i en miljö där samarbete är lika viktigt som kompetens, och där det du gör skapar mening.
   

  

**Din roll som Strategic Sourcing Manager**
 Inköps- och leverantörsstyrningsfunktionens uppdrag är att vara en gemensam, bolagsövergripande funktion som ska vara både stödjande och styrande för både inköps- och kontraktsförvaltningsprocessen.
   

  

**Huvudsakliga ansvarsområden:**
* Tillsammans med dina nio kollegor verka för att alla typer av inköp fungerar på ett effektivt sätt som ger bra pris och prestanda för Bankgirot.
* Driva inköpsrelaterade inköp – och upphandlingar samt tillhörande aktiviteter i enlighet med beslutade processer.
* Leverantörsstyrning: Säkerställa att valda leverantörer kvalificerar sig till de kriterier Bankgirot specificerat.
* Sammanställa och skicka RFI/RFP, leverantörsutvärderingar, inköpskalkyler, kontraktsförhandlingar samt stödja i implementationsprocessen.
* Aktivt arbeta med omvärldsbevakning av leverantörsmarknaden.
* Delaktighet i utformandet av leverantörsrelaterade avtalsmallar, metoder, riktlinjer samt inköp – och upphandlingspolicyn.
* Stötta vid förändringar av, mot kunden, betalmodeller, affärsmodeller eller leveransmodeller m.m. inom ramen för pågående upphandling.
* Samarbeta med interna intressenter och styrgrupper


**Vem är du?**
 Som en viktig samhällsaktör i en föränderlig bransch ställs vi ständigt inför nya utmaningar. Därmed tror vi också att du är en person som gillar att driva förändring i ett högt tempo och sprider energi och entusiasm för att bidra till Bankgirots förflyttning mot framtiden.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som är drivande, analytisk, strukturerad och en trygg, erfaren, kommunikativ inköpare. Du är bra på att skapa struktur och se möjligheter. Du är både operativ och strategiskt lagd. Du har en förmåga att motivera och utveckla både inköpskategorier samt processer.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har:
   

  

  

* Gedigen erfarenhet som Inköpare inom IT och inköpsstrategier inom IT-kategorier med fokus på hårdvara, mjukvara, licenser, molnlösningar, tjänster och outsourcing.
* Mångårig erfarenhet från Inköp- och leverantörsstyrning.
* Du är trygg och van att arbeta med komplexa upphandlingar.
* Du har tidigare arbetat i regulatoriska verksamheter som står under myndighetstillsyn.
* Du tycker om att utveckla och effektivisera inköpsprocesser.
* Du är van att arbeta i och driva flera projekt från ett inköpsperspektiv.
* Väldigt bra på att kommunicera med intressenter, leverantörer och ledning.
* Trygg i att upprätta och förhandla inköpsavtal.
* Du är en naturlig teamspelare och delar med dig av dina kunskaper.


 Meriterande:
   

  

  

* Jobbat inom försäkringssektor, finanssektorn eller statliga bolag
* Projektledning
* Revision och granskning
* Affärsjuridik


**Vi erbjuder**
 På Bankgirot har vi tillsammans byggt ett öppet, ansvarsfullt och affärsmässigt klimat där vi alla är med och påverkar, stöttar och växer med och av varandra och där vi aldrig kompromissar med vår etiska kompass. Vi bygger relationer som håller, både med varandra och med dem vi är till för.
   

  

 Hos oss handlar arbetslivet inte bara om prestation, utan också om att du ska trivas, utvecklas och känna dig hemma. Du får bra anställningsvillkor och förmåner som faktiskt gör skillnad i vardagen.
   

  

**Placering:**
 Vi sitter vid Liljeholmen med bra kommunikationsmöjligheter och förväntan är att arbetet sköts minst 4 dagar i veckan på kontoret. Tjänsteresor kan förekomma 3-5 ggr/år.
   

  

**Övrigt**
 För rollen genomförs säkerhetsprövning innan anställning i enlighet med säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) – en naturlig del av ansvaret att värna tryggheten i Sveriges betalsystem.
   

  

**Mer om Bankgirot**
 Sedan starten 1959 har vi stått för den tekniska strukturen som binder samman Sveriges banker i vårt nationella betalsystem. Vår historia är rik på innovation, engagemang och stolthet över vårt samhällsviktiga uppdrag. Genom vår expertkunskap och vår starka kultur, Bankgiroandan, arbetar vi varje dag för att säkerställa att varje transaktion sker sömlöst och effektivt.
   

  

 Tillsammans med våra kunder och samarbetspartners skapar vi resultat och långsiktigt värde som gynnar marknaden, samhället och Bankgirot. Som en förenande kraft gör vi det möjligt att forma en moderniserad och framtidssäkrad svensk betalinfrastruktur.
   

  

 Vi förenar, förvaltar och förnyar betalsverige! Läs mer på www.bankgirot.se
   

  

**Delar du vår inställning och har vi väckt ditt intresse?**
 I den här rekryteringen samarbetar vi med Wise.
   

  

 Har du frågor om tjänsten kan du kontakta ansvarig rekryteringskonsult Oscar Dahl (Oscar.Dahl@wise.se). Vi arbetar med löpande urval och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas före sista ansökningsdatum.
   

  

 Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!
   

  

 Bankgirot är ett svenskt clearinghus och teknikbolag med över 60 års erfarenhet av att leverera finansiell infrastruktur till banker. I samverkan med betalsektorn ser vi till att privatpersoner och företag enkelt kan göra överföringar och betalningar. Bankgirot säkerställer att det nuvarande betalsystemet fungerar felfritt, samtidigt som vi utvecklar framtidssäkrade lösningar. Vi säkrar och utvecklar Sveriges betalningar.
   

  

 Läs mer på www.bankgirot.se

title: Senior Sales Engineer - UPS Power
description:
**The Role**
**JOB DESCRIPTION**
 In the
 **Senior Technical Sales Engineer** 
 role, you will provide technical support for sales across various Lines of Business in Vertiv, working as a part of a large team that may interface with local and regional customers. In this role you will provide Sales with technical support in building proposals, quotes and tenders, with direct responsibility on local accounts or projects, but also enable the development of technically and price competitive applications for products to fulfil customer requirements where standard products do not fit.
   

  

 You will need to have the technical competence to understand and demonstrate the Vertiv offering and our capabilities for your specific segment. Your targets will be set to align with the growth expectations of the market and Vertiv strategic position with the respective accounts.
   

  

 Senior Technical Sales have a key role in Vertiv and consequently we are looking for a highly competent, energetic, and ambitious individual to take on this role within the organization, learn and develop skills across different products, lines of business, customers, and regions. You will be part of a large team of Application Engineers and Technical Sales Experts with clear competencies, career paths and opportunities to take your career to the next level.
   

  

**As a Senior Technical Sales Engineer, You Shall**
 Provide complex presales technical support by reviewing the RFx and identifying customer requirements/specifications.
   

  

 Coordinate with factory engineering departments for specific technical requests.
   

  

 Design and deliver
 **innovative** 
**solutions** 
 , rethinking current standards and routines to achieve competitive advantages in the market. Successfully initiate and sustain the necessary changes, managing internal stakeholders and securing support.
   

  

 Evaluate special requests that don’t fit standard solutions and propose complex customizable options (Configure to Order)
   

  

 Manage the relationship between the various stakeholder groups in Vertiv (Sales teams, Engineering Teams and others)
   

  

 Create and perform product and application presentations for customers and consultants.
   

  

 Provide internal trainings for colleagues in Vertiv and external trainings for customers
   

  

 Manage In-country activities (ICA), related to Application Engineering tasks
   

  

 Anticipate market trends being an Industry expert.
   

  

 Ensure that any activity, task, and responsibility required is managed and performed in full compliance with the Company
 **Code of Conduct** 
 and
 **Trade Compliance** 
 .
   

  

 Collect and act based on voice of the customers
   

  

 Provide feedback to the line of business in Vertiv on how to improve the offering
   

  

 Attend, facilitates and leads events for Vertiv with external customers
   

  

 Expert on multiple products and lines of business in Vertiv
   

  

**You Have**
 Solid technical background. Industry expertise or degree in electrical or mechanical engineering or equivalent.
   

  

 Engineering curiosity. Passion to learn every single day and stay ahead of the curve. You are results oriented.
   

  

 Ability to work effectively in the matrix organization
   

  

 Knowledge of the assigned segment and industry in EMEA.
   

  

 Excellent commercial experience to understand and anticipate market trends, and Vertiv’s business environment.
   

  

 Minimum of 5 years of proven records in Offering, Engineering, Pre-Sales, Product Marketing
   

  

 Communications and interpersonal skills
   

  

 You are fluent in English and your local language, and ideally speak a 2 nd European Language
   

  

 Excellent IT skills (Word/Excel/PowerPoint) and Customer/Sales orientation. CAD proficiency is an advantage.
   

  

 Previous working experience: experience in products and installation, especially for Datacentre or equivalent
   

  

**How Do We Set You Up For Success**
 You will become part of a best in class, diverse and vibrant sales community who truly enjoy growing together.
   

  

 From your day 1, you will access Vertiv Sales Academy training programs, accelerating your learning, and becoming an expert in our technology, industry trends and learning more on company processes, tools and technical skills.
   

  

 You will experience a knowledge sharing culture, also offering learning on the flow webinars, sales podcasts, drop-in learning sessions, along with continuous coaching opportunities and structured two-ways feedback.
   

  

 You will count on a deep collaboration with Sales and Engineering teams throughout the entire selling process.
   

  

 You may be required to travel abroad from time to time. Travel will occasionally require overnight stays.
   

  

 At Vertiv, we are a company engineered to deliver solutions for critical technologies. And those solutions often come from having a variety of viewpoints and perspectives at the table. So, it should come as no surprise that there's a spirit of inclusion found in everything we do. Having a wider range of experiences to draw from doesn't just make us a diverse company. It makes us a better company.
   

  

 Vertiv works hard to create a global workplace that supports and promotes diversity, embraces inclusion and cultivates respect for the individual. We believe that bringing together bright, enthusiastic, and talented people from various backgrounds promotes the birth of new ideas and an exciting energy level.
   

  

 We seek to create an employer of choice environment in terms of promotions, transfers, compensation, benefits, career mentoring, company-sponsored training, tuition assistance and social and recreational programs for our employees.
   

  

 Vertiv's Equal Employment Opportunity policy ensures that there will be no discrimination or harassment against an employee or applicant on the grounds of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, national origin, veteran status or any other factor considered unlawful by applicable laws and regulations.
   

  

**About The Team**
 Work Authorization
   

  

 No calls or agencies please. Vertiv will only employ those who are legally authorized to work in the United States. This is not a position for which sponsorship will be provided. Individuals with temporary visas such as E, F-1, H-1, H-2, L, B, J, or TN or who need sponsorship for work authorization now or in the future, are not eligible for hire.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity Employer
   

  

 We promote equal opportunities for all with respect to hiring, terms of employment, mobility, training, compensation, and occupational health, without discrimination as to age, race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy status (including childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression (including transgender status or sexual stereotypes), genetic information, citizenship status, national origin, protected veteran status, political affiliation, or disability.

title: Senior Data Scientist / AI Engineer - Generative AI
description:
**Job ID: 1878**
 We are looking for an experienced Senior Data Scientist / AI Engineer to build and scale Generative AI applications. The role is permanent based in Stockholm or Helsinki. We work in a hybrid model and expect team members to work three days per week in the office. This is an English-speaking working environment.
   

  

**About The Team**
 You will join Nordea’s AI & Machine Learning team, part of the group financial crime prevention (GFCP) unit that provides financial crime prevention services across Nordea. We use AI to improve how we detect and investigate financial crime across the bank, helping to keep our customers, our company, and society safe. We are a team of about 25 colleagues based in Stockholm, Helsinki and Copenhagen. We have already launched Nordea’s first generative AI product and are now building the next wave of high impact generative AI use cases.
   

  

 Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.
   

  

**Why this role is exciting**
 This is an opportunity to work on real-world Generative AI applications in a domain where quality, robustness, and responsible AI matter. You will help turn ideas into production-grade AI solutions in financial crime prevention, working closely with business stakeholders and experienced colleagues across the Nordics.
   

  

**In this role, you will**
* Identify and prototype high impact GenAI projects with business stakeholders
* Build, deploy and monitor production-grade AI applications
* Design robust evaluation approach to measure and improve application quality
* Help shape best practices, tooling and architecture for Generative AI development
* Support and mentor teammates through hands-on collaboration
* Document models and process to meet risk and compliance standards to ensure responsible and compliant use of AI


**Your background and skills:**
* MSc or PhD in Data Science, Machine Learning, Computer Science, Software Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Statistics, or a related field. Equivalent practical experience is also valued
* 5 years+ experience building and deploying production ML/AI systems in python
* 2 year+ experience developing generative AI applications, using LLM APIs, RAG, modern orchestration, agent-based patterns or other relevant technologies
* Strong systems design skills for ML & AI applications
* A proven track-record of turning ideas into prototype, and prototype into production
* Ability to evaluate and improve AI application quality using structured testing and experimentation
* Strong communication skills across technical and non-technical stakeholders


**What We Offer**
**People** 
 come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.
   

  

**A culture** 
 that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.
   

  

**Hybrid working model –** 
 we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusion** 
 are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.
   

  

 If this sounds like you, get in touch!
   

  

**Next steps**
 Submit your application no later than 21/06/2026. For more information you are welocome to contact Wen Zhou, Head of Unit, at wen.zhou@nordea.com. A Background Check will be performed for the person selected for this position, including internal applicants.
   

  

**We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.**
**We build relationships.** 
 We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.
   

  

**We learn and develop.** 
 We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.
   

  

**We lead change.** 
 We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.
   

  

**We are Nordea.** 
 We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.
   

  

**For candidates in Finland:** 
 A security clearance will be performed for the person selected for this position.
   

  

**For candidates in Sweden:** 
 For union information, please contact Finansförbundet at finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACO at SacoNordea@nordea.com.

title: Inköpschef
description:
**Inköpschef till AMF** 




*Har du ett närvarande ledarskap, en trygghet i inköpsprofessionen och trivs i en roll där du får kombinera operativt arbete med att utveckla team och arbetssätt? Vill du ta ansvar för en affärskritisk funktion i en regelstyrd samt finansiell och IT-tung verksamhet där ditt ledarskap och din förmåga att bygga förtroende verkligen gör skillnad?* 




  





**Om rollen** 




 Som inköpschef har du det övergripande ansvaret för AMFs inköpsfunktion och leder ett mindre, specialisttungt team. Du ansvarar för att utveckla och förvalta inköpsprocesser och arbetssätt som stödjer verksamhetens behov och gör det lätt att göra rätt. Rollen är operativ och innebär att du själv driver inköp, upphandlingar och förhandlingar i nära kontakt med verksamheten.
 



 Funktionen är etablerad i dag, men uppdraget innebär att se över inköpsstruktur, processer, mandat och ansvar där det finns behov av ökad tydlighet. Inköp är en central stödfunktion med stor påverkan på kostnader, risk och regelefterlevnad i en regelstyrd finansiell verksamhet, vilket gör rollen både affärskritisk och styrningsnära.
 



 Du är en närvarande chef med gedigen inköpskompetens som leder teamet i det dagliga arbetet genom att sätta riktning och tydliga prioriteringar. I rollen arbetar du nära flera delar av verksamheten, särskilt IT och juridik, och driver inköpsarbetet genom dialog, relevanta frågor och en förmåga att väga olika perspektiv.
 



 I ansvaret ingår även leverantörsuppföljning, avtalsförvaltning samt att föredra inköpsrelaterade frågor och vägval inför företagsledning och styrelse.
 



  





**Vi erbjuder dig** 




 Hos oss får du:
 


* Ett viktigt uppdrag – du bidrar till att skapa trygghet för miljoner människor i ett bolag där samhällsnytta och långsiktighet står i centrum.
* Möjlighet att forma och professionalisera inköpsfunktionen med tydligt mandat och närhet till ledning och styrning.
* En kultur som präglas av samarbete, prestigelöshet och korta beslutsvägar.








**Vem du är** 




 Vi tror att du har akademisk examen och att du har med dig mångårig erfarenhet av kvalificerat inköpsarbete. Du känner dig trygg i att ta ansvar för hela inköpsprocessen i sammanhang där ansvar, affär och regelverk behöver hanteras parallellt. Du har verkat i en regelstyrd miljö, gärna inom finansiell eller annan tillståndspliktig verksamhet, där inköps- och leverantörsarbete behöver förhålla sig till olika regulatoriska krav. Erfarenhet av exempelvis DORA eller liknande regelverk är relevant för rollen.
 



 Din bakgrund har gett dig god förståelse för hur struktur och kvalitet skapas även när allt inte är fullt ut definierat från början. Har du dessutom arbetat med IT-relaterade inköp och drivit inköpsfrågor i nära samspel med avtalsägare och systemägare ser vi det som ett plus, särskilt i tekniskt och organisatoriskt komplexa miljöer.
 



 Du har tidigare haft personalansvar och trivs i ett operativt chefsuppdrag där ledarskap och eget genomförande går sida vid sida. Som ledare är du närvarande och trygg, med förmåga att sätta tydliga ramar samtidigt som du skapar engagemang och delaktighet i teamet.
 



 Rollen innebär ett nära arbete med verksamheten och många kontaktytor, vilket ställer krav på god relationsförmåga och tydlig kommunikation. Du kan resonera kring vägval och presentera inköpsfrågor på ett förtroendeingivande sätt gentemot chefer, företagsledning och styrelse, och har ett helhetsperspektiv på hur inköp bidrar till stödjer verksamheten i stort. Du uttrycker dig väl på svenska och engelska i både tal och skrift.

title: Arbetsledare
description:
**Job Description**
**Om tjänsten**
 Sherwin-Williams är en global ledare inom färg- och ytbehandlingslösningar med en historia som sträcker sig över mer än 150 år. År 2010 förvärvades tidigare Becker Acroma som sedan dess ingår i Sherwin-Williamskoncernen. Globalt är vi över 63 000 medarbetare varav 330 i Sverige fördelade på fyra orter med tillverkning i Märsta och Bellö, centrallager i Nässjö samt med en helägd återförsäljare i Vaggeryd. I Sverige fokuserar vi på industriell ytbehandling av trä och metall där våra enheter utgör en central roll av vår totala försäljning i Europa.
   

  

 Nu söker vår produktionsanläggning i Märsta en erfaren Arbetsledare som vill vara med och driva vår produktion framåt. Rollen är en central del av vår dagliga drift och har ett tydligt ansvar för att skapa en trygg, effektiv och välplanerad arbetsmiljö där både människor och produktion kan utvecklas.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
**Din roll**
 Som arbetsledare har du en nyckelroll i att hålla ihop produktionen – både i det dagliga operativa arbetet och i den övergripande planeringen. Rollen säkerställer att produktionen har rätt förutsättningar att leverera enligt plan, följa upp avvikelser och samordna insatser i flödet.
   

  

 Du leder morgonmöten, följer upp planeringen och stöttar operatörer i operativa beslut. Du arbetar nära med övriga stödfunktioner i organisationen för att säkerställa trygg arbetsmiljö, hög kvalitet och kontinuerliga förbättringar.
   

  

 Tjänsten är en tjänstemannaroll med arbetstider från måndag-fredag. Du rapporterar till Operations Manager och har personalansvar upp till 25–30 medarbetare som sköter fyllning och internlogistik.
   

  

**Dina arbetsuppgifter:**
* Operativt ansvar för daglig styrning i produktionen.
* Personalledning och coachning.
* Beslutsstöd vid prioriteringar.
* Uppföljning av produktionsutfall och avvikelser.
* Säkerställa arbetsmiljörutiner och att processer följs.
* Ledning av dagliga pulsmöten och veckoproduktionsmöten.
* Arbete med ständiga förbättringar, ex: standardiserade arbetssätt.
* Samordning med produktion och stödfunktioner.


**Qualifications**
**Vem är du?**
 Du gillar att jobba där det händer saker och ser lösningar i stället för hinder. Du har en stark förmåga att planera, prioritera och följa upp, samtidigt som du inspirerar och engagerar dina medarbetare. Du är van att samarbeta med olika personer och avdelningar och trivs med att jobba i ett team.
   

  

 Som ledare är du framåt och tycker om att peppa och stötta andra – du vet att framgång skapas tillsammans! Du är en prestigelös lagspelare som ser helheten, bygger goda relationer och får saker att hända. Din positiva inställning smittar av sig och bidrar till ett öppet och inkluderande arbetsklimat. Vidare är du trygg och lösningsorienterad även när besluten behöver fattas under press.
   

  

**Kvalifikationer**
* 5–10+ års erfarenhet som arbetsledare eller någon typ av arbetsledning med personalansvar inom tillverkande industri.
* Gymnasieutbildning eller motsvarande.
* God datorvana.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, tal och skrift.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med 5S och följa SOP:er
* Kunna analysera data och ta beslut baserat på fakta.
* Erfarenhet av förbättringsarbete och LEAN.
* Ekonomisk förståelse.
* Det är mycket meriterande om du har arbetat inom kemi/färg.


**Vad vi erbjuder**
 Hos oss får du chansen till en spännande tjänst i ett stort internationellt företag med många kontaktytor och goda utvecklingsmöjligheter. Genom kollektivavtal, friskvårdsbidrag och andra förmåner är vi en arbetsplats som värdesätter din trygghet och ditt välmående.
   

  

 Observera att du kommer genomgå en nyanställningsundersökning inför anställning vilket bl.a. innefattar alkohol- och drogtest.
   

  

**Ansökan**
 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan! Har du frågor om tjänsten är du varmt välkommen att kontakta oss.
   

  

**About Us**
 At Sherwin-Williams, our purpose is to inspire and improve the world by coloring and protecting what matters. Our paints, coatings and innovative solutions make the places and spaces in our world brighter and stronger. Your skills, talent and passion make it possible to live this purpose, and for customers and our business to achieve great results. Sherwin-Williams is a place that takes its stability, growth and momentum and translates it to possibility for our people. Our people are behind the strength of our success, and we invest and support you in:
   

  

 Life … with rewards, benefits and the flexibility to enhance your health and well-being
   

  

 Career … with opportunities to learn, develop new skills and grow your contribution
   

  

 Connection … with an inclusive team and commitment to our own and broader communities
   

  

 It's all here for you... let's Create Your
 **Possible**
 Eligibility to Work
   

  

 You will need to provide proof of right to work. It is a condition of any offer of employment we make to you that you have the permission to work in the country for the role for which you are applying for.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity Employer
   

  

 An equal opportunity employer, all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment and will not be discriminated against based on race, colour, religion or belief, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, ethnic or national origin, disability, age pregnancy or maternity, marital or civil partner status, or any other protected characteristic prohibited by law.
   

  

 Please be aware, Sherwin-Williams recruiting team members will never request a candidate to provide a payment, ask for financial information, or sensitive personal information like national identification numbers, date of birth, or bank account numbers during the application process.

title: Breakfast Manager | Hôtel Reisen | Stockholm
description:
Hôtel Reisen, part of The Unbound Collection by Hyatt, is a historic waterfront hotel in Stockholm’s Old Town dating back to 1617. We blend modern luxury with deep-rooted heritage, offering our guests premium accommodations, Restaurant Reiss, an intimate spa, and a vibrant jazz bar. 
 



  





**THE ROLE:** 




 We are looking for a hands-on, service-driven Breakfast Manager to lead our morning team. In this role, you’ll set the tone for our guests' day, ensuring everything from the buffet presentation to the morning welcome is seamless. You will manage daily operations, lead the front-of-house team, and collaborate closely with our Breakfast Chef and F&B management to deliver an exceptional morning experience. 
 



  





**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:** 



* Team Leadership: recruit, onboard, schedule, and develop the breakfast team while maintaining a positive work environment and respecting Swedish labor laws.
* Daily Operations: lead breakfast shifts from the floor, engaging with guests and handling special dietary needs or feedback.
* Quality & Presentation: oversee the daily setup and replenishment of the buffet to ensure freshness and high visual standards.
* Stock & Costs: manage inventory, place vendor orders, minimize waste, and keep operations aligned with our budget.
* Compliance: maintain strict compliance with hygiene, HACCP, and food safety regulations, alongside Hyatt brand standards.



  





**WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:** 



* Previous supervisory or management experience in hotel breakfast, restaurant, or F&B operations (a luxury or boutique hotel background is a plus).
* A natural leader who genuinely loves hospitality and thrives in early morning environments.
* Solid organizational skills with experience in inventory, scheduling, and cost control.
* Fluency in English; Swedish is a strong advantage.
* Familiarity with POS systems and scheduling software.
* Valid eligibility to work in Sweden.



  





**WHAT WE OFFER:** 



* The chance to lead a key department in a unique historic property backed by the global Hyatt network.
* A supportive, professional, and collaborative team culture.
* Great career development opportunities and standard Hyatt employee benefits.



  





**HOW TO APPLY:** 




 If you’re ready to help us shape the start of every guest's day, we’d love to hear from you. Please click
 **"** 
 Apply
 **"** 
 to submit your CV or email it directly to restaurant@hotelreisenstockholm.com with "Breakfast Manager" in the subject line. We review applications on a rolling basis, so feel free to apply early!

title: Staff Software Engineer (L4) - Back End (Bangkok based, Relocation provided)
description:
**About Agoda**
 At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.
   

  

 Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.
   

  

 No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.
   

  

**Get to Know Our Team**
 Fintech is one of the fastest growing areas in Agoda and we are rapidly expanding our tech team. We work closely with the finance business team and our Fintech product owners to reduce risk, drive efficiencies and move on new market opportunities in this exciting field. We have a wide range of projects from traditional finance to cutting-edge customer fintech. For example, reconciliation using Big Data technologies, growing and optimizing customer payments options, tax calculations in milli-second response times and a mesh of supplier payment options like virtual credit cards. It’s a hot field and the perfect mix of data engineering and backend engineering.
   

  

**The Opportunity**
**In this role, you will not only develop robust backend systems but also architect and maintain scalable data pipelines and storage solutions that support complex data collection, processing, and analysis. Your dual expertise in backend and data engineering will play a crucial role in optimizing our financial technology solutions and driving informed business decisions through reliable data insights.**
**In This Role, You’ll Get to**
* Think and own the full life cycle of our products, not just a single piece of code – from business requirements, technology selection, coding standards, agile development, unit and application testing, to CI/CD and proper monitoring
* Design, develop and maintain platforms and data pipelines across fintech
* Boost System Performance: build systems that are stable, scalable, and highly performant to meet the dynamic demands of the financial landscape
* Write great code and help others write great code – mentor people in your team and wider
* Collaborate with other teams and departments
* Exceptional problem-solving skills coupled with a strategic mindset are essential. You possess the ability to adapt to new changes and the foresight to anticipate future needs. Leadership at Agoda isn’t just managing tasks but inspiring innovation and driving vision into reality
* Foster Cross-Functional Collaboration: work with diverse teams to drive forward product and technology goals
* Shape our future team: Play a pivotal role in recruiting and onboarding exceptional talent


**What You’ll Need To Succeed**
* 8+ years of experience with strong proficiency in Java, Kotlin, Scala, or C# with a proven track record of developing high-performance applications in production settings. Insightful experience with big data technologies like Hadoop, real-time processing frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark), and advanced knowledge of SQL and data architecture
* Thinks in systems: their edge cases, failure modes, and life cycles
* Uses a metrics driven approach and can make informed decisions using data
* You are passionate about the craft of software development and constantly work to improve your knowledge and skills
* Experience with Scrum/Agile development methodologies
* Excellent verbal and written English communication skills
* Experience with operational excellence and a deep understanding of metrics, alarms and dashboards


**It’s Great If You Have**
* Experience working in a modern FinTech or Payments organization
* Domain knowledge in any of these areas: financial reconciliation, financial reporting, tax, payout methods like virtual credit cards or customer payments
* Hands-on experience working with technologies like Spark for data processing, ETLs for data pipelines and queueing systems (Kafka, RabbitMQ)
* Core engineering infrastructure tools like GitLab for source control and Continuous Integration, Kubernetes
* Experience developing, maintaining and debugging large-scale distributed systems
* Experience in leading projects, initiatives and/or teams, with full ownership of the systems involved


**This position is based in Bangkok, Thailand (Relocation Provided)**
 #Bengaluru #Hyderabad #Gurgaon #NewDelhi #Seoul #SouthKorea #Berlin #London #Pakistan #Pune #SanFrancisco #SanJose #Seattle #TelAviv #Jakarta #Colombia #NewYork #Vietnam #Russia #Madrid #SaoPaulo #Noida #Toronto #Chennai #Singapore #Ukraine #UAE #Sunnyvale #BuenosAires #Austin #Barcelona #Nigeria #Mumbai #Paris #Egypt #Stockholm #Amsterdam #Romania #Serbia #MountainView #Chile #Warsaw #Sydney #Bangladesh #Peru #SantaClara #Chicago #Gurgaon #Munich #Montreal #Bangkok #Tallinn #Vilnius #Riga #Dublin #IT #ENG #5
   

  

 Please review our Hiring Process Guidelines before your interview — click here to learn how interviewing at Agoda works.
   

  

**Discover More About Working At Agoda**
* Agoda Careers https://careersatagoda.com
* Facebook https://www.facebook.com/agodacareers/
* LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/agoda
* YouTube https://www.youtube.com/agodalife


**Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Agoda, we pride ourselves on being a company represented by people of all different backgrounds and orientations. We prioritize attracting diverse talent and cultivating an inclusive environment that encourages collaboration and innovation. Employment at Agoda is based solely on a person’s merit and qualifications. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunity regardless of sex, age, race, color, national origin, religion, marital status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, citizenship, veteran or military status, and other legally protected characteristics.
   

  

 We will keep your application on file so that we can consider you for future vacancies and you can always ask to have your details removed from the file. For more details please read our privacy policy.
   

  

**Disclaimer**
 We do not accept any terms or conditions, nor do we recognize any agency’s representation of a candidate, from unsolicited third-party or agency submissions. If we receive unsolicited or speculative CVs, we reserve the right to contact and hire the candidate directly without any obligation to pay a recruitment fee.

title: Channel Manager - Reader portfolio      .
description:
Channel Manager - Reader portfolio
   

  

 Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

 32 - 38 hrs
   

  

 What are you going to do?
   

  

 As
 **Channel Manager for Nordics, Baltics, Central & Eastern Europe** 
 , you are responsible for driving the growth of Nedap’s Reader portfolio across the region. You build strong relationships with OEMs, distributors, and system integrators, helping them create secure, future-proof access management solutions.
   

  

 Our Reader portfolio plays a key role in Nedap’s Full Access Management strategy within Security. With a strong position in long-range identification, RFID, vehicle identification, and multi-technology reader solutions, we support organizations worldwide in securing people, buildings, and assets. As part of Nedap in Security, the Reader portfolio further strengthens our position in the growing access management market and creates new opportunities to combine hardware, software, and cloud-based services into one integrated solution.
   

  

 You own the regional channel pipeline and translate market opportunities into sustainable growth. Together with our partners, you identify new opportunities, support deal execution, and strengthen Nedap’s position within the access control ecosystem.
   

  

 At Nedap, we believe technology starts with people. That means you are not only focused on commercial success, but also on creating long-term value for customers and partners. You work closely with colleagues from product management, marketing, and technical support to deliver solutions that truly make an impact.
   

  

 As part of Nedap in Security, you contribute to our ambition to strengthen our leading position in access management through innovative Digital Twin Technology solutions.
   

  

 Start your application
   

  

 Your responsibilities
   

  

 Develop and execute the regional channel strategy for the Reader portfolio across Nordics, Baltics, and Central & Eastern Europe
   

  

 Build and strengthen relationships with OEMs, distributors, and system integrators
   

  

 Manage and grow the regional sales pipeline, from opportunity identification to deal closure
   

  

 Support partners during commercial and technical sales processes
   

  

 Identify market trends, customer needs, and growth opportunities within the access control ecosystem
   

  

 Work closely with product management, marketing, and technical support teams
   

  

 Represent Nedap at customer meetings, partner events, and industry exhibitions
   

  

 Contribute to the continued growth and positioning of Nedap in Security
   

  

 Your team
   

  

 You become part of Nedap in Security, one of Nedap’s four key markets. Our Security business unit develops access management solutions that help organizations worldwide secure people, buildings, and assets. We combine decades of expertise in access control with innovative cloud and Digital Twin Technology solutions.
   

  

 Within the team, entrepreneurship and ownership are central. You work in an international environment with colleagues who combine technical expertise with a strong commercial mindset. Together, we continuously improve our solutions and strengthen our market position.
   

  

 Our offer
   

  

 What's in it for you?
   

  

 At Nedap, we offer more than just a job. You become part of a people-first organization where you get the freedom and responsibility to make an impact.
   

  

 You can expect:
   

  

* An international role with a high level of autonomy and ownership
* The opportunity to work with innovative access management technologies
* A collaborative and entrepreneurial work environment
* Plenty of room for personal and professional growth
* A competitive salary and attractive secondary benefits
* A hybrid way of working with flexibility and trust


 All terms of employment
   

  

**Required Skills**
 You are a commercially driven professional with extensive experience in security and access management. You know how to build strong relationships with channel partners and customers, manage complex sales processes, and identify growth opportunities in an international market.
   

  

* Minimum of 10 years of experience in B2B sales or channel management within security, access control, or related technology markets
* Experience working with OEMs, distributors, or system integrators
* Strong network within the regional physical security market
* Entrepreneurial, proactive, and independent way of working
* Affinity with technology and access management solutions
* Excellent communication skills in English; additional regional languages are a plus
* Willingness to travel regularly within the region
* Preferably based in the Nordics


 Get in touch!
   

  

 Interested in the vacancy
 **Channel Manager - Reader portfolio** 
 or curious about what it’s like to work at Nedap? Feel free to contact Paulette Kamperman via paulette.kamperman@nedap.com. We’re happy to help!

title: Manager Nordic & Baltics Business Development (13 month FTC)
description:
**Our Purpose**
*Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.*
**Title And Summary**
 Manager Nordic & Baltics Business Development (13 month FTC)
   

  

 The Role
   

  

 This role is offered on a 13-month fixed-term contract to provide maternity cover.
   

  

 As a manager, you will be instrumental in driving our new revenue streams and the growth of our new customer segments. You will play a key role in developing new revenue streams and setting the strategy for these new segments, including Retail & Commerce, public sector, and more. You will be responsible to develop and implement effective sales strategies to reach our targets.
   

  

 You will be responsible for managing and expanding existing customer relationships with selected Nordic and Baltic strategic accounts, tapping into new markets/buying centres to generate and qualify leads, promote products and services, and drive sales.
   

  

 In addition, you will contribute to new client business development by initiating contact with potential new customers in new segments and managing opportunities through the sales pipeline to a successful close, further extending our customer base and revenue.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Develop and implement strategies to grow new revenue streams and enter new segments such as Retail & Commerce and the public sector.
* Exhibit strong leadership and relationship management skills to drive new revenue growth and expand new customer segments and fostering lasting partnerships with new clients.
* Be detail-oriented, forming a deep understanding of clients’ strategies, objectives, operations, leaders, etc.
* Have a customer-first attitude, capable of uncovering customer needs and crafting innovative customer strategies.
* Take ownership of the sales process, from prospecting to executing commercial contracts, ensuring a seamless and efficient sales cycle.
* Be responsible for achieving sales targets, revenue goals, and sales projections, consistently delivering outstanding personal performance.
* Collaborate closely with other team members to move sales processes into signed deals.
* Foster a one team-oriented environment within the sales group and collaborate effectively with other colleagues across Mastercard to drive collective success.


**All About You**
* Demonstrated excellence and career development in a B2B sales role.
* Proven self-starter with a record of success in a team-oriented environment.
* Expertise in selling to big logos at least some of the following industries: retail, travel, hospitality, telco, restaurants, consumer goods companies and the public sector
* B2B sales experience preferably in data/analytics/insights, loyalty, or professional services.
* Proven experience developing re-seller business and working with channel partners in the sales process.
* Demonstrated experience in selling solutions to C-level clients.
* Strong communication and persuasion skills, both written and oral.
* Ability to influence internal and external stakeholders across markets and divisions.
* High level of energy, drive, enthusiasm, initiative, and commitment.


**Corporate Security Responsibility**
 All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:
   

  

* Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
* Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
* Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
* Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Partner Manager
description:
*Vill du driva affärer, bygga nya partnerskap och ha en nyckelroll i att utveckla ett av Sveriges ledande upplevelsebolag?* 




  





**Om Live it** 




 Live it är ett av Sveriges ledande upplevelsebolag och erbjuder ett brett utbud av upplevelser inom allt från äventyr och mat till avkoppling och unika minnen. Genom ett starkt nätverk av upplevelseleverantörer skapar vi minnesvärda gåvor och aktiviteter för både privatpersoner och företag.
 



  





 Bolaget befinner sig i en fas där partnernätverket och produktutbudet utvecklas och expanderas, med ett tydligt fokus på tillväxt, kvalitet och starka samarbeten. Live it är en entreprenöriell organisation där affärsmässighet, kundnytta och utveckling står i centrum.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som Booking & Partner Manager har du en nyckelroll i att både utveckla och förvalta Live its nätverk av upplevelseleverantörer. Du arbetar nära affären och ansvarar för att identifiera, kontakta och etablera nya samarbeten samtidigt som du säkerställer att befintliga partners fungerar optimalt i hela kedjan från avtal till levererad upplevelse.
 



  





 Rollen kombinerar aktivt säljarbete och affärsutveckling med operativt ansvar för bokningar, avtal och produktinformation. Du är en central kontaktpunkt för partners och arbetar nära interna funktioner såsom försäljning, marknad, ekonomi och kundservice för att säkerställa effektiva flöden och hög kvalitet i leveransen.
 



  





 Det här är en roll för dig som trivs i ett högt tempo, gillar att skapa nya affärer och samtidigt har förmågan att arbeta strukturerat och noggrant med detaljer som gör skillnad i den dagliga driften.
 



  





**Exempel på ansvarsområden** 



* Vara drivande i ny partnerförsäljning genom uppsökande försäljning, nätverkande och prospektering
* Identifiera och etablera nya upplevelseleverantörer som stärker Live its erbjudande
* Ansvara för hela säljprocessen från första kontakt till signerat avtal
* Bygga och utveckla långsiktiga relationer med befintliga och nya partners
* Hantera onboarding av nya partners inklusive avtal, prissättning och kommersiella villkor
* Ansvara för bokningsflöden, tillgänglighet och löpande bokningsadministration
* Följa upp leverans, kvalitet och kundupplevelse kopplat till partners
* Analysera bokningsdata och identifiera trender och förbättringsområden
* Säkerställa hög kvalitet i partner- och produktinformation
* Hantera avvikelser, reklamationer och operativa partnerfrågor
* Samarbeta tätt med interna team för att utveckla processer och förbättra kundupplevelsen



  





**Din profil** 




 Vi söker dig som trivs i en kommersiell och relationsintensiv roll där du får kombinera försäljning, affärsutveckling och operativt ansvar. Du är självgående, initiativrik och har ett starkt driv att skapa nya affärer och bygga långsiktiga samarbeten. Du är trygg i att ta kontakt med nya människor, trivs med uppsökande försäljning och har en naturlig förmåga att skapa förtroende. Samtidigt är du strukturerad, noggrann och har lätt för att hantera flera parallella processer med bibehållen kvalitet. Du motiveras av att arbeta nära affären, se resultat av ditt arbete och bidra till både tillväxt och förbättrade arbetssätt i en dynamisk miljö.
 



  





**Kvalifikationer** 



* Erfarenhet av B2B-försäljning, affärsutveckling eller partneransvar
* Vana att arbeta med hela säljprocessen från prospektering till avtal
* God administrativ förmåga och erfarenhet av operativt arbete i system
* Erfarenhet av kund- eller partnerrelationer i en kommersiell miljö
* God analytisk förmåga och vana att arbeta med data och uppföljning
* God systemvana, gärna erfarenhet av affärs- eller bokningssystem
* Flytande svenska och engelska i tal och skrift
* Erfarenhet från upplevelsebranschen, turism eller e-handel är meriterande



  





**Ansökan**

title: Account Manager Existing Business (1 year maternity cover)
description:
**This job is with Unit4, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.**
 Meet Unit4 . With over 40 years of heritage, we’re an agile, fast‑growing cloud company on a mission to redefine Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) for mid‑market, people‑centric organisations.
   

  

 With our innovative, self‑driving, adaptive, and intuitive software, our customers can spend more time on meaningful, high‑value work.
   

  

 At the heart of what we do lies a simple yet profound purpose: to improve how people work by focusing on what truly matters — a powerful idea that supports different priorities for different people.
   

  

 We’re shaping how work should feel, and we empower our people with the right tools and trust to achieve real autonomy. That’s what makes Unit4 different.
   

  

 Discover how we’re transforming work — and how people experience it — so our customers and their people can truly thrive.
   

  

 We have an exciting
 **maternity cover** 
 opportunity for a customer-focused Existing Business Account Manager who is passionate about delivering value and supporting long-term customer success.
   

  

 In this role, you will support Unit4’s Cornerstone Niche Product portfolio, primarily UPM, FPM, and MAP, with scope to cover additional niche solutions over time. These products are deployed both as standalone solutions and integrated with the Unit4’s ERP technology stack. You will work closely with customers, internal teams, and partners to drive adoption, retention, and sustainable commercial outcomes.
   

  

 Your mission is to protect and grow recurring revenue (ARR), deliver ACV quota for the assigned territory, and support customers in their digital and cloud transformation journeys, maximising the value they gain from our solutions.
   

  

 This is a Account Manager role, ideal for someone with solid experience in managing existing customer accounts who enjoys balancing relationship management, commercial ownership, and partner collaboration.
   

  

 This is a hybrid role based in Portugal or Sweden.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
 Account Ownership & Customer Value
   

  

* Own and manage a defined portfolio of existing customers using Cornerstone niche products.
* Build trusted relationships with key customer stakeholders, acting as their primary commercial point of contact.
* Understand customer objectives and usage to position relevant upgrades, add-ons, and services that support digital and cloud transformation


 ARR Retention & Commercial Performance
   

  

* Drive ARR retention through proactive account management, renewal preparation, and early risk identification.
* Achieve ACV quota for renewals, upsell, and cross-sell within the assigned territory.
* Identify churn risks and coordinate mitigation plans together with Customer Success, Support, and Product teams.


 Sales Execution (Existing Business)
   

  

* Manage the end-to-end sales cycle for renewals, upsell, and cross-sell within your customer base.
* Apply a structured and repeatable sales approach aligned with Unit4’s ways of selling.
* Collaborate with pre-sales, product, engineering, and delivery teams as needed.


 Partner Management
   

  

* Manage and support sales and implementation partners aligned to UPM, FPM, MAP, and related solutions.
* Coordinate jointly with partners on sales opportunities, renewals, and customer success initiatives.
* Ensure partners are aligned with Unit4 commercial objectives, solution value propositions, and customer outcomes.


 Collaboration & Internal Alignment
   

  

* Work closely with Product, Support, Customer Success, and Professional Services to ensure a consistent and positive customer experience.
* Feed customer insights, roadmap input, and market feedback back into the organisation.
* Maintain accurate pipeline, forecast, and account information in CRM.


 Qualifications
   

  

**About You**
* Experience as an Account Manager or similar role managing existing customers within SaaS, ERP, or enterprise software.
* Background in ERP, financial systems, or adjacent enterprise applications is an advantage.
* Understanding of subscription-based business models, including renewals and ACV targets.
* Experience working with partners (sales and/or implementation), ideally in an ecosystem-driven environment.
* Comfortable discussing digital and cloud transformation in a customer-relevant, outcome-focused way.


**You Will Also Embody And Promote Our SaaS Behaviours**
* One Team – You foster collaboration and shared accountability across functions
* Customer Centricity – You prioritise customer outcomes in every decision
* Continual Learning – You embrace curiosity and growth
* Standardised Scalability – You build repeatable, scalable processes
* Ongoing Innovation – You drive continuous improvement and experimentation
* Data-Informed – You make decisions grounded in insights and analytics


 Additional Information
   

  

**Who We Are**
 We are a people-first community that nurtures all the areas that surround your working experience. With us, you’ll be surrounded by a high-performance team that supports your authentic self and celebrates your uniqueness.
   

  

 We believe that ‘How work should feel’ is an evolving statement. Work goes beyond tasks and everyday responsibilities it’s about feeling valued, empowered, promoted, impactful, seen, and appreciated.
   

  

 We are reimagining how work makes people feel.
   

  

**What We Offer**
* a culture built on trust and accountability - giving you the freedom and autonomy to be successful and make an impact
* balance - with our Flexible Leave Paid Time Off policy, remote working opportunities, Global Wellbeing Days, and other great benefits
* growth opportunities - we provide the tools and guidance required so that you can focus on what really matters to you and so, ultimately, you can achieve your best work
* talented colleagues , role models and mentors - work, learn and be inspired by some of the best talent in the software industry
* a commitment to sustainability - with initiatives such as our Environmental, Social, and Governance strategy and Act4Good programme
* a safe and inclusive working environment – supported by our Employee Resource Groups, which are open to all


**Qualifications**
 Unit4 is committed to ensuring equal opportunity for everyone. We make our hiring decisions solely based on skills, qualifications, and our current business needs. We know that diversity brings fresh perspectives, ideas, and solutions to our company. This is the essence of our culture. We also welcome and encourage people who are pregnant and/or parents-to-be to apply. If you would like to know more about our commitment to diversity, visit our blogs:
   

  

* Our focus to DEI remains central to who we are
* Inclusive hiring at Unit4: Building equal opportunities for all


**Salary range: 536,996 - 595,235 SEK per år**

title: Legal Counsel
description:
We are looking for a skilled
 **Legal Counsel** 
 to join a cross-functional team where legal plays a key role in driving outcomes, shaping strategy, and supporting complex case handling in a fast-paced, regulated environment.
   

  

 You will be part of a collaborative team supporting customer-related matters in a structured and fast-moving environment. The role combines legal analysis and close collaboration with business and operational stakeholders to deliver pragmatic, commercially sound outcomes.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Act as a trusted legal advisor in a cross-functional setup handling complex and escalated matters.
* Contribute legal analysis, strategy and execution in disputes and litigation matters.
* Collaborate closely with business, operations, and other stakeholders to deliver balanced, pragmatic, and legally sound solutions.
* Support the continuous development of processes, tools and ways of working.


**We Are Looking For You Who**
* Holds a Swedish or Finnish law degree.
* Has at least 3 years of relevant legal experience from a law firm and/or an in-house role.
* Thrives in a cross-functional, project-based environment.
* Provides clear, pragmatic and solution-focused legal advice.
* Communicates fluently in Swedish or Finnish, as well as English.
* Experience in litigation and/or consumer credit law is considered a plus.


**To thrive and perform well in this role, we believe you are:**
 To thrive and perform well in this role, we believe you are a driven and proactive Legal Counsel who is not afraid to roll up your sleeves and take ownership. You are a structured and detail-oriented team player with an open mindset, who enjoys working across functions and managing multiple parallel cases in a dynamic environment. You are hands-on and take initiative to drive work forward and ensure progress.
   

  

 In addition to this, it is important to us that you recognize yourself in our values: we are wholehearted, we collaborate and we take the lead.
   

  

**Practical Information**
